EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S001 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 The corresponding increase in expertise needed to be able to perform these procedures and maintain quality outcomes should be considered
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S002 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 DIAGNOSTIC WORK-UP AND IMAGING 17 Recommendation : Diagnostic work-up and imaging Recommendations Levelb Clinical risk assessment Preoperative risk assessment is I B [159–162] TTE is recommended as part of the preoperative work-up
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S003 General Coronary Revascularization - - reduce the risk of a missed aneurysm - critical (1.00) 0.54 Thus, these software tools should be inserted into the clinical workflow, if possible, even if none are available for aortadedicated imaging, to reduce the risk of a missed aneurysm
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S004 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.58 In the presence of complications, such as malperfusion and rupture, urgent surgery is recommended, equal to that for type A aortic dissection
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S005 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 BAV plus other risk factors), surgery can be considered at >_50 mm even for the ascending phenotype: Factors to consider include growth rate, family history, hypertension, younger age, short stature and patient’s willingness to be operated on earlier [141, 339] (‘recommendations for root and ascending aorta’)
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S006 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 Whereas the smooth muscle cells 38 In patients with HTAD attributable to a pathogenic variant in PRKG, surgery on the aortic root or ascending aorta may be considered at a diameter of >_42 mm
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S007 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 If endovascular treatment is likely to achieve a favourable result in the presence of high-risk features, lower thresholds are reasonable
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S008 General Coronary Revascularization - an incomplete circle of Willis diagnosed in preoperative coronary CTA - - critical (1.00) 0.62 In patients with an incomplete circle of Willis diagnosed in preoperative coronary CTA, trilateral antegrade perfusion may be considered by additional cannulation or intubation of the LSA
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S009 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 IIa C Hybrid procedures may be considered for aortic arch repair
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S010 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.58 Aortic centres should represent the standard of care for acute aortic syndrome
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S011 General Coronary Revascularization - - guide clinical practice, their application should always be tailored to the needs of the individual patient - critical (1.00) 0.54 However, in essence, although these recommendations serve as a valuable resource to guide clinical practice, their application should always be tailored to the needs of the individual patient
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S012 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 Furthermore, these guidelines should not be interpreted as legally binding documents
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S013 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 Is recommended/is indicated Class II Conflicting evidence and/or a divergence of opinion about the usefulness/efficacy of the given treatment or procedure
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S014 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 May be considered Class III Evidence/general agreement that the given treatment/procedure is not useful/effective and may sometimes be harmful
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S015 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.58 I C - The use of the TEMd classification should be considered in any acute aortic syndrome to determine the type of disease and an initial treatment strategy
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S016 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.58 IIa C - The use of the GERAADAe score should be considered in patients with acute type A aortic dissection undergoing surgery to determine 30-day mortality
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S017 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 This proposed wording should help create a better understanding of the pathophysiology as well as help standardize communication among physicians describing the pathological categories
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S018 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 All other procedures on the arch should be named partial arch replacement
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S019 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 These characteristics should be clearly separated from an earlier version of a non-A non-B dissection defined by an entry tear in the arch with retrograde extension, which is nothing but an overlap and subset of type A dissection [49]
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S020 General Coronary Revascularization - - The use of Ishimaru zones - critical (1.00) 0.62 The use of Ishimaru zones is recommended when reporting on both open and endovascular aortic treatment extension, AD extension (such as aneurysm or dissection) and location of communications between aortic lumens in patients with an aortic dissection
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S021 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 Therefore, the TEM classification or the descriptive approach defining the dissection anatomy according to Ishimaru zones (for example, aortic dissection with entry in zone 0 extending down to zone ) is recommended to be used in clinical practice and in the literature
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S022 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 Most recently, a large group of international experts developed a consensus statement on nomenclature and classification of BAV and the associated aortopathy: The valve should be described as ‘fused’, ‘2-sinus’ or ‘partial-fusion’ type, whereas the aortopathy has been classified as ‘root phenotype’ (15–20% cases, dilatation prevailing at the level of the sinuses), ‘ascending phenotype’ (70–75%, dilatation prevailing at the tubular tract) or ‘extended phenotypes’ (5–10%, either root dilatation with significant extension into the tubular tract or tubular dilatation involving also the proximal arch) [80]
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S023 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 the root phenotype (or the root extended) should undergo stricter surveillance and an earlier operation
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S024 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 Not only should the intervened-upon segment be followed for healing or disease progression, pseudoaneurysm, endoleak, stent-induced new entry or antegrade/retrograde dissection, but the native aorta, whether normal or residually diseased (e.g
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S025 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 Further study is required, but, eventually, regional specialization for the treatment of AD, while maintaining adequate access to care, may optimize outcomes, especially for highmortality conditions and high-risk procedures
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S026 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 / European Journal of Cardio-Thoracic Surgery Recommendation : Aortic teams and healthcare implications Recommendations Classa Levelb Refc Shared decision-making for the optimal treatment of aortic pathologies by a multidisciplinary aortic team is recommended
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S027 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 I C - In patients with multisegmental aortic disease, treatment is recommended in aortic centres providing open and endovascular cardiac and vascular surgery on site
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S028 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 I C - Transfer to an aortic centre should be considered for patients with complex aortic pathologies
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S029 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 IIa B [105–118] For endovascular aortic procedures, a hybrid operating room, including an integrated imaging system, is recommended
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S030 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 The ultimate aim of an aortic team should be to provide a comprehensive care model designed to streamline an earlier and more accurate diagnosis and to implement appropriate timely therapy, including optimal medical therapy, open surgical intervention or an endovascular procedure and ultimately to produce optimal clinical outcomes
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S031 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 Ultimately, the aortic team should embrace a multidisciplinary approach according to the patient’s needs, specific aortic segment involved, clinical presentation, proposed therapeutic options and estimated risks according to the experience of the surgical team [137–140]
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S032 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.62 An aortic team should encompass a variety of expert specialists as a comprehensive service line whose knowledge and expertise are available on a 24/7 basis to treat all types of acute and chronic thoracic and abdominal aortic conditions in elective and emergency settings, using surgical and endovascular interventions
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S033 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 Although the patient with AD can be initially evaluated at every layer of the health-care system, core members of the aortic team should include cardiovascular surgeons, cardiologists and radiologists because all have relevant roles in the treatment of AD [128, , 143]
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S034 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 Moreover, for the routine practical approach to the patient and for specific clinical questions, anaesthesiologists, perfusionists, critical care specialists, geneticists, genetic counsellors, neurologists, pulmonologists, renal specialists, specialized nurses and social workers should also be considered as part of the aortic team
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S035 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 Standard operating procedures of the aortic team, including a regular meeting schedule and agenda and a regional network organized around a specific aortic centre (AC) should be defined locally [105]
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S036 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 Patients with AASs who present outside regular work hours must be assessed by the on-call specialists
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S037 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 Although there is some debate as to the optimal manner in which to regionalize care in patients with AD [134], the existence of an AC has merit and should be balanced against the need for optimal timing in the treatment of aortic pathology for maximal patient benefit [53]
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S038 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 Several characteristics have been described that should distinguish an AC from its institutional counterpart, which include providing the highest-level quality of patient care, innovation through research, education of both learners and colleagues and defining standards of care [108]
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S039 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 Distinguishing features of a comprehensive AC should encompass the following:
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S040 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 / European Journal of Cardio-Thoracic Surgery Recommendation : Diagnostic work-up and imaging Recommendations Classa Levelb Refc Clinical risk assessment Preoperative risk assessment is recommended
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S041 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 I B [159–162] TTE is recommended as part of the preoperative work-up
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S042 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 I B [163–166] Coronary evaluation/assessment should be considered to rule out coronary artery disease
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S043 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 IIa B [167] Imaging—morphology and function As the first line diagnostic modality, CTA from the common carotid to the common femoral arteries is recommended for imaging the aorta and its pathologies
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S044 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 I C - For repetitive imaging of the aorta, it is recommended that the same imaging modality be used, with a similar method of measurement to assess change in diameter
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S045 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 I C - Assessment of patency and morphology of the circle of Willis should be considered prior to total aortic arch replacement
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S046 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 IIa C - In patients with known or suspected aortic disease, it is recommended that aortic diameters be measured at reproducible anatomical landmarks (and its maximal diameter) perpendicular to the axis of blood flow
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S047 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 I C - In asymmetric or oval contour cases, reporting the longest diameter and its perpendicular diameter should be considered
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S048 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 IIa C - When performing CT or MRI, one should consider measuring the aorta from the outer edge to the outer edge, if the vessel wall is visible
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S049 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.58 However, beyond this clinical risk score, a constellation of first-line simple examinations, including the electrocardiogram (ECG), the chest X-ray, TTE and biomarkers, is performed primarily in patients with chest pain in the emergency room and can be useful to rule out alternative diagnoses (e.g
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S050 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 Otherwise, TOE can be performed in the operating room for type-A aortic dissection because it is useful to complete the information provided by TTE+CT
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S051 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 Of note, invasive therapeutic options should be weighted individually in each patient
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S052 STEMI - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 Assessing baseline neurologic function is important for the initial risk stratification, and systemic cognitive assessment by experts should be part of the evaluation
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S053 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 A prior stroke could contribute to frailty and therefore should be added to the frailty index used to assess the patient’s ability to perform independent daily activities
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S054 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.58 A preoperative workup that includes pulmonary function tests may be beneficial, and blood work (including kidney and liver function tests) is necessary for patients undergoing any elective aortic procedure [175]
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S055 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 An invasive coronary angiogram is recommended in patients with known coronary artery disease, whereas non-invasive testing, such as cardiac CT, may be sufficient in selected patients without symptoms of coronary artery disease [175]
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S056 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 These variables should be considered for stratification of surgical risk, particularly in elderly patients
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S057 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 Evaluation of both is essential before One should consider measuring the aortic root from sinus to sinus, and the largest diameter measured should be considered as a reference value
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S058 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 IIa C - Assessment of the risk of radiation exposure is recommended, especially in younger adults and in those undergoing repetitive imaging
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S059 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 I C - For CT or MRI techniques involving the entire aorta, it is recommended that motion artefacts of the ascending aorta be minimized and that the entire aorta should be acquired in a single data set
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S060 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 In case of planned procedures on the aortic arch, the circle of Willis should also be imaged to assess its morphology and patency
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S061 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 The supply is based on 2 essential interrelated components that must be assessed before each procedure: (i) the 4-territory concept that describes the large extraspinal inflows, comprising the left subclavian, intercostal, lumbar and internal iliac arteries [176]
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S062 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 Although low, the radiation dose can accumulate with repeated imaging and should be prevented in young patients by using radiation-free modalities
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S063 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 As a minimum standard for imaging of the aorta, an isotropic resolution of 1 mm should be available in the CT scanner system used
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S064 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 For imaging the aortic root and the ascending aorta, ECGtriggered and/or fast acquisition techniques are recommended to minimize pulsation artefacts and increase measurement accuracy and diagnostic reliability
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S065 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 Nevertheless, these techniques should only be used if they do not negatively impact the minimal requirements (e.g
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S066 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 A contrast-enhanced arterial-phase series extending from the thoracic inlet to the level of the femoral artery is recommended in most cases
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S067 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 although the radiologist should be aware of this association while reading the CT scan, the scientific evidence is not strong enough to recommend a screening test for an intracranial aneurysm in patients with an aortic aneurysm
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S068 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 further, a portal venous-phase series can be useful to determine malperfusion of the abdominal organ
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S069 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 Also, a native CT series should be performed prior to the arterial-phase series at the first postoperative scan to reliably distinguish foreign material from contrast agent
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S070 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 Similar to CT, ECG-triggered acquisition should be chosen if the aortic root and ascending aorta require imaging
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S071 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 Therefore, MRI is often the first choice for the evaluation of congenital aortic malformations and is recommended for serial imaging in young patients or in patients in whom a radiation dose should be avoided (e.g
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S072 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 For agents with the lowest risk (like macrocyclic agents), assessment of renal function prior to MR scanning is not mandatory, and a patient should be encouraged not to refuse a clinically well-indicated enhanced MR examination to avoid a contrast agent [191]
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S073 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 It is useful in the evaluation of complications such as aortic regurgitation, left ventricular (LV) dysfunction and cardiac tamponade
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S074 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 It is useful in the longitudinal monitoring of the aortic root and ascending aortic dilatation, provided these aortic segments are well visualized
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S075 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 Intravascular ultrasound is useful in guiding the endovascular treatment of complex lesions of the thoraco-abdominal aorta because, in addition to assessing the landing zone, it can visualize aortic size, tortuosity, plaque burden, calcification, vascular branching and endovascular filling defects (e.g
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S076 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 Ultrasound is recommended for screening and monitoring of an abdominal aortic aneurysm [192], which is defined as >30 mm in diameter, using primarily an anterior–posterior diameter, and using the outer edge-to-outer edge measurement
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S077 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 Measurements should be performed perpendicular to the long axis of the aorta at specific segments, usually 8 measurements in the thoracic aorta (Fig
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S078 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 The diameter should be derived from cross-sectional images
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S079 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 In the past, it was argued that one should measure the aortic diameter from inner-edge to inner-edge as long as no wall changes were present
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S080 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 discrete thickening from atherosclerosis, IMH, aortitis or other processes), maximum diameter should be measured including the aorta wall using the outer-to-outer edge method
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S081 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 Not surprisingly, the aortic dimensions vary also across the heart cycle, which should be considered in the report and in the longitudinal analyses
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S082 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 The centreline should be parallel to the potential blood flow, in the middle of the true lumen and perpendicular to the cross-sectional measurements
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S083 General Coronary Revascularization - - improve the risk stratification (‘see above’) - critical (1.00) 0.54 The length of the ascending aorta illustrates the natural history of the ascending aortic aneurysm, and its measurement should be used to improve the risk stratification (‘see above’)
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S084 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 Nevertheless, the length of the ascending aorta should be part of the imaging reports (Fig
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S085 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 Improved visualization of the acquired image data and novel access to this data are required to extract the maximum information from these data and make them understandable for the benefit of the patient
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S086 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 A targeted systolic pressure of 100–120 mmHg and a heart rate of 60–80 bpm are recommended [208, 209]
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S087 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 Adequate invasive monitoring should be provided in an intermediate or ICU setting
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S088 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 Still, evaluation of each patient’s condition must weigh the risk of surgery—with all potential adjuncts necessary—against optimal medical therapy
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S089 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 Clinical assessment must not be forgotten or neglected in the current high-tech imaging climate
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S090 Revascularization Strategy - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 A revascularization-first approach using endovascular fenestration or stenting is associated with lower in-hospital mortality and may be considered in patients with radiographic and clinical evidence of mesenteric malperfusion [229, 230]
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S091 General Coronary Revascularization - circumferential detachment or complete avulsion of the coronary artery - - critical (1.00) 0.70 In patients with circumferential detachment or complete avulsion of the coronary artery, coronary Recommendation : Acute aortic diseases: Type A aortic dissection Recommendations Classa Levelb Refc Initiation of emergency surgery is recommended in patients presenting with acute type A aortic dissection
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S092 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 I B [211, 212] A tear-oriented approach with exclusion or resection of the primary entry tear in the ascending aorta and arch is recommended
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S093 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 I B [213, 214] Inspection and coverage of communications between lumina in the proximal descending aorta may be considered in specialized centres for prognostic reasons
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S094 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 IIb C - Despite preoperative neurologic dysfunction or non-haemorrhagic stroke, open repair should be considered
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S095 Revascularization Strategy - - revascularization - critical (1.00) 0.62 IIa B [215–217] In case of clinical and imaging evidence of visceral malperfusion, revascularization may be considered prior to aortic repair
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S096 STEMI - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 IIb C - Antegrade systemic perfusion via axillary or direct aortic cannulation should be considered
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S097 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 IIa B [218, 219] An open distal anastomosis during lower body hypothermic circulatory arrest is recommended
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S098 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 In case of paraplegia due to spinal cord ischaemia, cerebrospinal fluid (CSF) drainage should be considered as the patient is prepared for ascending aortic replacement
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S099 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 due to the unpredictable behaviour of the dissected aorta (in particular in the vicinity of the aortic valve, coronary arteries and supra-aortic branches), swift repair is warranted, considering a mortality risk of .5% per hour while waiting for surgery [223]
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S100 General Coronary Revascularization - undergoing surgical aortic repair - - critical (1.00) 0.62 In patients undergoing surgical aortic repair, the axillary artery should be used for arterial inflow
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S101 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 In addition, a tear-oriented approach, consisting of resection or exclusion of the primary entry tear, is recommended [213, , 221]
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S102 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 Inspection of the proximal descending aorta during hypothermic circulatory arrest should be considered
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S103 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 In case of entry tears in the distal arch or proximal descending aorta, implantation of an FET may be considered [241]
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S104 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 As an alternative, post-surgical TEVAR may be considered for entry tear coverage and optimization of true lumen perfusion [136]
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S105 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 IIa C - In patients with anatomical feasibility to cover the primary entry tear, a stent graft implantation may be considered
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S106 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.58 / European Journal of Cardio-Thoracic Surgery Recommendation : Acute aortic diseases: type B aortic dissection Recommendations Classa Levelb Refc In patients with complicated acute type B aortic dissection and suitable anatomy, TEVAR is recommended
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S107 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.58 I B [249–253] In patients with acute complicated type B aortic dissection with unsuitable anatomy for TEVAR, FET repair should be considered
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S108 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.58 IIa B [254, 255] In acute type B aortic dissection with high-risk features, TEVAR should be considered in the subacute phase
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S109 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.58 IIa C - In patients with acute type B aortic dissection without high-risk features, optimal medical therapy, close monitoring and follow-up is recommended for emerging high-risk features
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S110 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 This strategy may be considered for patients with a large ascending aorta or aortic arch, retrograde extension of a type B dissection and lack of an adequate landing zone for an endovascular intervention and for patients with connective tissue disorders [247, , , 280]
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S111 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 I B [283, 287–290] Optimal medical therapies and serial imaging may be considered in patients with type A IMH in the absence of high-risk features
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S112 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.58 IIb C - In selected patients with acute type A IMH without high-risk features but a tear in the descending aorta, TEVAR may be considered in addition to OMT in specialized centres
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S113 General Coronary Revascularization - acute complicated type B IMH - - critical (1.00) 0.70 In patients with acute complicated type B IMH, urgent aortic repair is indicated
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S114 General Coronary Revascularization - unsuitable anatomy - - critical (1.00) 0.62 In patients with unsuitable anatomy, open surgery should be performed [305]
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S115 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 In the absence of complications, medical management is recommended in the initial phase
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S116 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 Open descending aortic replacement should also be regarded as an alternative for the treatment of type B IMH, especially in cases of vasa vasorum rupture because pain relief in this setting is debatable after TEVAR
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S117 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.58 / European Journal of Cardio-Thoracic Surgery : High-risk features in intramural haematomas Age >70 years [293, 294] Initial aortic diameter >45 mm [293, 295] Mean aortic diameter growth rate >_5 mm/year [296] Wall thickness of involved segment >_10 mm [297] Pleural effusion based on Hounsfield units [298, 299] Presence of aortic ulcer or ulcer-like projection [294, 300] Recommendation : Acute aortic diseases: type B intramural haematoma Recommendations Classa Levelb Refc In patients with acute complicated type B IMH, urgent aortic repair is recommended
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S118 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.58 I B [305] In patients with acute type B IMH with highrisk features, TEVAR should be considered if the patient is anatomically suitable
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S119 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.58 IIa C - In patients with acute type B IMH without high-risk features, OMT and serial imaging are recommended
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S120 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.58 I B [305, 306] In patients with acute type B IMH without high-risk features, OMT, close monitoring and follow-up are recommended
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S121 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 I B [289] In patients with high-risk PAUs located in the distal arch or descending aorta, TEVAR should be considered if anatomically suitable
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S122 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 IIa B [310] In patients with high-risk PAUs located in the distal arch or descending aorta unsuitable for TEVAR, open surgical repair should be considered after careful evaluation of operative risk
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S123 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 Behind this clinical background, procedural risk of endovascular or open surgery should be weighed against frailty, comorbidities and life expectancy in this patient cohort
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S124 General Coronary Revascularization - symptomatic - - critical (1.00) 0.58 Therefore, symptomatic PAUs or PAUs with high-risk features located in the ascending aorta should undergo urgent surgical repair
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S125 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 In addition, high-risk features for malignant courses of any PAU should be evaluated () [305]
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S126 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 In high-risk PAUs distal to zone , aortic repair should be performed
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S127 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 BTAI involving the ascending aorta or the aortic arch is rare and should be treated based on individual decision-making of the aortic team
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S128 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 Therefore, physicians should have a high index of suspicion based on the preclinical situation and the type of traumatic event
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S129 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 Thoracic endovascular aortic repair is the first choice if treatment is indicated and in the presence of suitable anatomy [141]
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S130 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 For grade II BTAI lesions that present with high-risk imaging features, TEVAR should be considered the treatment strategy of choice
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S131 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 Grade III–IV lesions should be managed operatively with TEVAR
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S132 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.58 / European Journal of Cardio-Thoracic Surgery Recommendation : Acute aortic diseases: blunt traumatic aortic injury Recommendations Classa Levelb Refc In patients with BTAI grade I, nonoperative management, including close follow-up imaging, is recommended
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S133 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 I C - In patients with BTAI grade II and high-risk imaging features, TEVAR should be considered
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S134 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 IIa C - In patients with BTAI grade II without highrisk imaging features, nonoperative management and close follow-up imaging may be considered
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S135 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 IIb C - In patients with BTAI grades III–IV and suitable anatomy, TEVAR is recommended
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S136 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 I B [333, 334] Surgery for BAV-related aortopathy with ‘root phenotype’ is recommended when the maximum aortic diameter is 50 mm
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S137 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 I B [82, 84–87, 335] Surgery for TAV-associated aneurysms with ‘root phenotype’ should be considered when the maximum aortic diameter is 50 mm in a low-surgical-risk setting.d IIa B [87, 336–338] In patients with low surgical riske and ‘ascending phenotype’ dilatation, both with TAV and BAV, surgical treatment should be considered when the maximum aortic diameter is >52 mm
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S138 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 IIa C - Surgery for aortic dilatation in patients undergoing aortic valve surgery should be considered at a root or ascending diameter >_45 mm
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S139 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 IIa C - Surgery for aortic root dilatation in patients undergoing surgery for ascending aortic aneurysm should be considered at an aortic root diameter 45 mm
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S140 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.58 IIa C - In patients with young age, a family history of the acute type A aortic dissection or known HTAD who are undergoing ascending replacement, a lower threshold than 45 mm may be considered for concomitant root replacement on an individual basis
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S141 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 To define the optimal timing for an operation in terms of diameter, the risk of complications in the natural history must be weighed against the operative risk: In this respect, important advancements in knowledge, through large observational studies, have taken place in the last decade
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S142 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 It has been emphasized that real rapid growth, as commonly defined, is rarely observed: Measurement errors must be ruled out before indicating earlier surgery based solely on this criterion [14, 348]
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S143 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.58 Despite established diameter criteria, studies [350] demonstrated that the majority of acute aortic dissections occur at a diameter <55 mm, suggesting that Laplace’s law probably applies to the risk of rupture but not to the risk of dissection: Different forces must evidently lead to the 2 different modes of aortic wall acute mechanical failure, transmural rupture versus intimal delamination [351, 352]
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S144 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 As an ascending aorta dilates and/or elongates, it must change its radius of curvature, assuming a C-shape, because the upper and lower ends are fixed
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S145 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 Therefore, in therapeutic planning, the diameter should be considered in the broad context of patient-specific factors including aortic length and angulation, age, sex, family history, genetic variants, and so forth, but also, importantly, aortic pain, i.e
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S146 General Coronary Revascularization - symptomatic - - critical (1.00) 0.58 / European Journal of Cardio-Thoracic Surgery Recommendation : Chronic aortic diseases: maximum diameter and maximum length Recommendations Classa Levelb Refc Surgery should be considered for a symptomatic aneurysm of the ascending aorta, almost independently of size (once nonaortic causes have been eliminated)
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S147 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 IIa C - For individuals of small body size, height nomograms should be considered in the decision about indications for surgery at various aortic dimensions
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S148 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.58 IIa B [340] An ascending aortic length exceeding 110 mm should be considered as a risk factor for aortic events when indicating elective surgery for aortic aneurysms
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S149 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 IIa B [196] Despite metrics and precision criteria, the benefits and risks of surgical intervention versus nonoperative management should be considered on a case-by-case basis
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S150 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 IIa C - Other non-diameter criteria that also bear on the risk of aortic events may be considered in the decision-making
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S151 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 aortic repair should be considered
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S152 General Coronary Revascularization - arch pathologies and diseased aortic segments distal to zone - - critical (1.00) 0.62 In patients with arch pathologies and diseased aortic segments distal to zone , FET repair should be considered
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S153 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 IIa B [311, 356–358] In patients with an indication for aortic repair unsuitable for open surgery, hybrid or endovascular aortic repair may be considered
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S154 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 IIb C - Hybrid or endovascular aortic repair is recommended to be performed in experienced centres with an adequate volume of both open and endovascular aortic repairs
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S155 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 Larger aortic diameters at the distal landing zone are unfavourable for successful endovascular treatment, and, given current device sizes, the distal descending thoracic aorta should be 40 mm or less to be able to treat the patient with 10–20% oversizing
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S156 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 Although the maximum aortic diameter in the thoracic aorta is usually closely monitored until it has reached the threshold for intervention, attention should also be paid to the total aortic diameter at the coeliac artery, which may already have reached 40 mm or greater before the maximum aortic diameter has reached 55 mm
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S157 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 Heritable thoracic aortic disease Recommendation : Heritable thoracic aortic disease Recommendations Classa Levelb Refc Genetic testing is recommended in patients with thoracic aortic disease <60 years of age, family history of TAD, arterial aneurysms in other segments and those with syndromic features
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S158 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 I B [169, , 373] Testing of family members is recommended by simpler, more cost-efficient Sanger sequencing of only the suspect genetic area
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S159 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 I C - Marfan syndrome In patients with Marfan syndrome, surgery on the aortic root or ascending aorta is recommended at a diameter of >_50mm
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S160 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 I B [374, 375] In patients with Marfan syndrome and high-risk features,* surgery on the aortic root or ascending aorta should be considered at a diameter of >_45 mm
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S161 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 IIa B [374, 375] In patients with Marfan syndrome without high-risk features with a high likelihood of undergoing valve-sparing aortic root replacement and very low surgical risk, surgery on the aortic root or ascending aorta may be considered at a diameter of >_45 mm when performed by an experienced aortic team
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S162 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 IIb C - In patients with Marfan syndrome, surgery of the aortic arch, descending thoracic aorta or abdominal aorta should be considered at a diameter of >_50 mm of the respective aortic segment
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S163 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.58 / European Journal of Cardio-Thoracic Surgery Recommendation : Chronic aortic diseases: chronic aortic dissection Recommendations Classa Levelb Refc Intervention is recommended in patients with chronic aortic dissection at a maximum aortic diameter of >_55 mm without involvement of the ascending aorta
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S164 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.58 I B [362, 363] In patients with HTAD with chronic aortic dissection, intervention at diameters <55 mm should be considered if the multidisciplinary aortic team makes the decision depending on the genotype, growth rate, family history and other individual patient risk factors
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S165 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 IIa C - In patients with dSINE, treatment is recommended to prevent diameter progression
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S166 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.58 I C - Intervention at >50 mm should be considered in patients with chronic aortic dissection if the treatment includes a multistep procedure, such as arch replacement with FET followed by TEVAR
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S167 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 / European Journal of Cardio-Thoracic Surgery Loeys–Dietz syndrome In patients with Loeys–Dietz syndrome, indication for surgery is recommended based on the specific genetic variant, aortic diameter, aortic growth rate, family history, history of aortic events, patient age and other individual patient-related factors and discussed by a multidisciplinary aortic team
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S168 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 I C - In patients with Loeys–Dietz syndrome attributable to a pathogenic variant in TGFBR1 or TGFBR, surgery on the aortic root or ascending aorta is recommended at a diameter of >_45 mm
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S169 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 I C - In patients with Loeys–Dietz syndrome attributable to a pathogenic variant in TGFBR1 and high-risk features,* surgery on the aortic root or ascending aorta may be considered at a diameter of >_40 mm
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S170 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 IIb C - In patients with Loeys–Dietz syndrome attributable to a pathogenic variant in TGFBR2 and high-risk features,# surgery on the aortic root or ascending aorta should be considered at a diameter of >_40 mm
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S171 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 IIa C - In patients with Loeys–Dietz syndrome attributable to a pathogenic variant in TGFB, surgery on the aortic root or ascending aorta may be considered at a diameter of >_50 mm
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S172 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 IIb C - In patients with Loeys–Dietz syndrome attributable to a pathogenic variant in SMAD, surgery on the aortic root or ascending aorta should be considered at a diameter of >_45 mm
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S173 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 IIa C - In patients with Loeys–Dietz syndrome attributable to a pathogenic variant in TGFBR, TGFBR2 or SMAD, surgery to replace the intact aortic arch, descending aorta or abdominal aorta at a diameter of >_45 mm may be considered
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S174 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 IIb C - In patients with Loeys–Dietz syndrome attributable to a pathogenic variant in SMAD2 or TGFB, surgery on the aortic root or ascending aorta may be considered at a diameter of >_45 mm
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S175 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 IIa C - In patients with HTAD attributable to a pathogenic variant in ACTA2 and high-risk features,* surgery on the aortic root or ascending aorta may be considered at a diameter of >_42 mm
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S176 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 Indication for surgery should be based on careful review of the individual patient by the multidisciplinary aortic team
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S177 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 / European Journal of Cardio-Thoracic Surgery In patients with HTAD attributable to a pathogenic variant in PRKG, surgery on the aortic root or ascending aorta may be considered at a diameter of >_42 mm
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S178 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 IIb C - In patients with HTAD attributable to a pathogenic variant in PRKG1 and high-risk features,* surgery on the aortic root or ascending aorta may be considered at a diameter of >_40 mm
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S179 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 IIb C - In patients with HTAD attributable to a pathogenic variant in MYLK or MYH, surgery on the aortic root or ascending aorta may be considered at a diameter of >_45 mm
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S180 General Coronary Revascularization - symptomatic - - critical (1.00) 0.54 I C - In patients with asymptomatic descending aortic aneurysms with high-risk features, repair should be considered in diameters <55mm
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S181 General Coronary Revascularization - symptomatic - - critical (1.00) 0.54 I C - In patients with asymptomatic thoracoabdominal aneurysms with high-risk features, aortic repair should be considered with diameters <55 mm
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S182 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 Hence, an open surgical or endovascular treatment should be undertaken when the risk of intervention is lower than the risk of rupture, which would cause death when untreated
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S183 General Coronary Revascularization - - reduce aneurysm growth (e - critical (1.00) 0.54 Before reaching the threshold for surgical intervention to prevent the risk of rupture, medical therapy should be adopted to reduce aneurysm growth (e.g
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S184 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 However, in patients with high-risk AAA, earlier intervention may be reasonable [426]
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S185 General Coronary Revascularization - symptomatic / symptomatic reduce the risk of rupture [192, 412–417, , 426] - critical (1.00) 0.70 In patients with symptomatic, unruptured AAA, urgent repair is indicated to reduce the risk of rupture [192, 412–417, , 426]
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S186 General Coronary Revascularization - ruptured AAA - - critical (1.00) 0.66 In patients with ruptured AAA, emergency surgical or endovascular treatment is indicated [192, 410– , , 426]
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S187 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.58 Because of the elective and preventive nature of any AAA intervention, stringent prior diagnosis of any comorbidities is recommended to determine the risk of intervention
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S188 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.58 Aortic Recommendation : Chronic aortic diseases: abdominal aorta Recommendations Classa Levelb Refc In patients with ruptured AAA, emergency surgical or endovascular treatment is recommended
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S189 General Coronary Revascularization - symptomatic - - critical (1.00) 0.58 I C - In patients with symptomatic, unruptured AAA, emergency repair is recommended, irrespective of diameter
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S190 General Coronary Revascularization - symptomatic - - critical (1.00) 0.54 I B [410, 411] In patients with asymptomatic AAA, repair is recommended when the AAA exceeds 55 mm
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S191 General Coronary Revascularization - symptomatic - - critical (1.00) 0.54 I A [192, 412–420] In women with asymptomatic AAA, repair should be considered when the AAA exceeds 50 mm
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S192 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 IIa C - AAA repair should be considered if aneurysm growth exceeds 10 mm/ year
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S193 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 IIa C - In AAA patients with high-risk features, earlier intervention at lower diameters may be considered
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S194 General Coronary Revascularization - symptomatic - - critical (1.00) 0.54 IIb C - In asymptomatic patients with AAA below the threshold for intervention, lifestyle modification, OMT and continuous surveillance are recommended
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S195 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.58 I C - In elective patients undergoing AAA repair, preoperative cardiac evaluation is recommended
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S196 General Coronary Revascularization - CAD - - critical (1.00) 0.58 I C - In elective patients undergoing AAA repair, preoperative cardiac echocardiography and/or screening for CAD (coronary artery angiography or coronary CTA) may be considered
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S197 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 Surgical results, usually with an aorto-bifemoral Y prosthesis implant, are excellent and should be considered as therapy of first choice in patients with extensive disease patterns such as TASC (Trans-Atlantic Inter-Society Consensus Document on Management of Peripheral Arterial Disease) C and D lesions [430]
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S198 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 / European Journal of Cardio-Thoracic Surgery Recommendation : How to monitor end-organ function and how to avoid end-organ injury Recommendations Classa Levelb Refc Perfusion In patients undergoing aortic surgery with involvement of the aortic arch, the preference for antegrade arterial inflow cannulation as compared to retrograde inflow cannulation should be considered
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S199 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 IIa B [218, , 432–435] Axillary artery cannulation should be considered for an antegrade inflow cannulation site for patients undergoing aortic surgery with involvement of the aortic arch
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S200 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 IIa B [218, , 432–435] Continued Multiple arterial pressure monitoring lines (upper and lower body) are recommended for aortic arch and/or thoraco-abdominal aortic repair
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S201 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 A femoral arterial line is recommended for lower body perfusion-pressure monitoring
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S202 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 I C - Temperature management Nasopharyngeal and core temperature (rectal/bladder) measurements are recommended in patients undergoing aortic arch surgery in lower body HCA
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S203 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 I C - Considering the core temperature (rectal/bladder) as the reference value for the determination of the level of lower body HCA is recommended
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S204 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 I C - A target hypothermic circulatory arrest temperature should be determined based on the anticipated extent of repair, expected duration of lower body HCA and presence of preoperative malperfusion
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S205 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 IIa C - The use of the term ‘mild hypothermia’ is recommended for core temperatures >28
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S206 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 I C - The use of the term ‘high moderate hypothermia’ is recommended for core temperatures between .1 C and
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S207 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 I C - The use of the term ‘low moderate hypothermia’ is recommended for core temperatures between .1 C and
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S208 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 I C - The use of the term ‘deep hypothermia’ is recommended for core temperatures <_20
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S209 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 I C - High-moderate lower body HCA in combination with cerebral perfusion for hemiarch replacement is recommended
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S210 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.58 I C - High-moderate lower body HCA in combination with selective antegrade cerebral perfusion for complex aortic arch procedures should be considered
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S211 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 IIa B [178] Target lower body HCA temperature, as well as the cerebral protection method, should be determined based on the anticipated extent of repair, expected duration of lower body HCA and the presence of preoperative malperfusion
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S212 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 IIa C - Organ protection—heart Retrograde cardioplegia should be considered to facilitate repetitive cardioplegia applications without interrupting the procedure
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S213 General Coronary Revascularization - - reduce myocardial ischaemia time - critical (1.00) 0.54 IIa C - Non-cardioplegic myocardial perfusion may be considered during extended aortic arch repair to reduce myocardial ischaemia time
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S214 General Coronary Revascularization - - the use of bilateral NIRS-based cerebral oximetry combined with an algorithmic approach to intervention for cortical Hb-desaturation - critical (1.00) 0.62 Some centres also use the carotid artery for arterial inflow, Organ protection—brain During surgery for aortic arch repair, the use of bilateral NIRS-based cerebral oximetry combined with an algorithmic approach to intervention for cortical Hb-desaturation is recommended
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S215 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 I C - In a patient with an incomplete circle of Willis diagnosed in preoperative cerebral CTA, trilateral antegrade perfusion should be considered via additional cannulation or intubation of the left subclavian artery
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S216 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 IIa C - Organ protection—spinal cord CSF drainage for spinal cord protection is recommended in open TAAA replacement
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S217 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 I B [436, 437] CSF drainage should be considered in total aortic arch replacement in patients at risk of SCI
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S218 General Coronary Revascularization - symptomatic - - critical (1.00) 0.54 IIa C - CSF drainage may be considered in type A aortic dissection patients with preoperative symptomatic SCI
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S219 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 III C - For patients at an increased risk of spinal cord injury undergoing endovascular treatment of thoracic or thoraco-abdominal aortic disease, prophylactic CSF drainage should be considered
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S220 General Coronary Revascularization - - reduce procedure-induced deprivation of antegrade arterial spinal cord supply to a minimum - critical (1.00) 0.54 IIa C - The 4-territory concept should be considered during planning to reduce procedure-induced deprivation of antegrade arterial spinal cord supply to a minimum
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S221 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 IIa C - A critical appraisal of the contribution of thoracic and lumbar segmental arteries to spinal cord perfusion should be considered before surgery to determine the need for reimplantation
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S222 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 IIa C - Prevention of steal in particular from segmental arteries to avoid SCI is recommended
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S223 General Coronary Revascularization - - reduce postoperative visceral, renal and spinal ischaemia - critical (1.00) 0.54 I C - Organ protection—visceral and renal In patients undergoing aortic arch surgery repair, early reinstitution of distal aortic perfusion should be considered to reduce postoperative visceral, renal and spinal ischaemia
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S224 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.58 IIa B [438–441] Selective visceral and renal perfusion is recommended in open TAAA repair I B [442–445] In TAAA surgery, maintenance of lower limb perfusion to avoid rhabdomyolysis and AKI is recommended
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S225 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 I C - Deep HCA should be considered in patients undergoing open thoracoabdominal aorta repair based on surgical and institutional experience
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S226 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 / European Journal of Cardio-Thoracic Surgery A period of hypothermic perfusion at the desired target core temperature before hypothermic lower body circulatory arrest should be considered to ensure appropriate thermal distribution throughout the body
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S227 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 IIa C - A period of hypothermic perfusion before initiation of the rewarming phase should be considered to decrease end-organ injury
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S228 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 IIa C - Arterial CPB outlet temperature of <37 C during rewarming to avoid hyperthermic perfusion is recommended
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S229 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 A standardized approach to temperature measurement and reporting in future studies is warranted to allow for more accurate comparisons between studies
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S230 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 However, it must be critically questioned whether 43 M
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S231 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 In general anaesthesia, the following 3-pillared lung-protective ventilation strategy should be applied: (i) moderate hyperoxia (FiO2 not exceeding 80%)
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S232 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 Retrograde flushing may be considered to clear any debris from the carotid arteries
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S233 General Coronary Revascularization - undergoing hemiarch replacement or other arch intervention - - critical (1.00) 0.62 In patients undergoing hemiarch replacement or other arch intervention with circulatory arrest times <40 min, both unilateral and bilateral antegrade perfusion may be reasonable
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S234 General Coronary Revascularization - undergoing longer circulatory arrest periods (>40 min) - - critical (1.00) 0.62 In patients undergoing longer circulatory arrest periods (>40 min), bilateral cerebral perfusion from the beginning is reasonable [496–499]
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S235 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 Nevertheless, it should be considered in high-risk patients with an already reduced spinal cord blood supply (previous TEVAR/ TAR), occluded LSA or internal iliac arteries
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S236 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 Satisfactory oxygen delivery and perfusion pressure should be maintained intraoperatively (before, during and after CPB) and postoperatively to avoid end-organ injury in aortic surgery
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S237 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 During CPB it is imperative to maintain a satisfactory goal-directed perfusion pressure, always ensuring appropriate pump flow and satisfactory oxygen content (CaO), and these goals should be pursued postoperatively
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S238 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 Avoidance of rapid rewarming and hyperthermic perfusion are recommended
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S239 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 To attenuate the rewarming insult, it is recommended to avoid temperature differences between the arterial and venous lines above 3 C, with a rewarming rate no faster than 1 C every 5 min
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S240 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 Leg ischaemia leading to rhabdomyolysis in TAAA surgery is associated with AKI and should be avoided, when possible, through lower limb perfusion 46 M
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S241 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 Ischaemic markers such as lactate level and lactate/pyruvate ratio should be monitored intraoperatively
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S242 General Coronary Revascularization - - reduce the risk for end-organ complications - critical (1.00) 0.54 In these cases, preemptive protective measures should be considered in order to reduce the risk for end-organ complications
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S243 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 In addition, the target activated clotting time should be achieved and monitored throughout the procedure
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S244 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 Early introducer sheath removal or downsizing with suture-mediated closure devices or purse-string sutures should be done, when possible, to increase peripheral blood flow [557]
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S245 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 In ATAAD, the general principle of surgical treatment is to resect the segment containing the entry tear: The root must be replaced when it is either the site of the intimal tears or significantly involved by dilatation
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S246 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 root dimensions) should prompt concomitant root replacement in ATAAD without a tear in the sinuses
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S247 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 / European Journal of Cardio-Thoracic Surgery Recommendation : Therapeutic options: aortic root Recommendations Classa Levelb Refc For aortic dilatations and aneurysms involving the aortic root with a structurally diseased aortic valve, replacement of the aortic valve and sinuses with coronary ostia direct reimplantation (modified Bentall operation) is recommended
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S248 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 I B [559–561] Valve-sparing root replacement should be considered for patients with a non-diseased tricuspid aortic valve and dilated root, especially young patients, if performed by experienced surgeons
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S249 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 IIa B [562, 563] Valve-sparing root replacement may be considered for patients with a nondiseased bicuspid aortic valve and dilated root if performed by surgeons with specific expertise in aortic valve repair
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S250 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 At least in patients with HTAD, the remodelling technique without annuloplasty should not be preferred because of concerns about late postoperative annular enlargement and recurrence/occurrence of aortic insufficiency [568, 569]
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S251 General Coronary Revascularization - undergoing surgery for an aneurysm of the midascending aorta - - critical (1.00) 0.62 In patients undergoing surgery for an aneurysm of the midascending aorta, a non-dilated or just mildly dilated (<_45 mm) root (‘ascending phenotype dilatation’) should not be replaced, unless there are concerns about the patient’s young age, family history of ATAAD or known HTAD
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S252 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 Moreover, ascending grafts should be chosen, anticipating further distal endovascular treatment: first, straight implantation of the prosthesis and second, a prosthesis length of >_7 cm
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S253 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 Aortic arch Recommendation : Ascending aorta with or without proximal arch Recommendations Classa Levelb Refc In ascending aortic surgery, supracommissural isolated ascending aorta replacement is recommended in patients without adjunct aortic root and aortic arch dilatation
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S254 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 I B [571, , 574] It is recommended that in any open proximal thoracic aortic operation, ascending/hemiarch replacement has to be extensive, and short ascending grafts should be avoided to prevent disease progression and to anticipate a future endovascular modular distal extension
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S255 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 I C - In ascending aortic surgery with a diameter larger than 45 mm of the proximal aortic arch, hemiarch or more extensive aortic arch replacement may be considered
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S256 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 IIa B [576–581] Aortic arch replacement, including the creation of a sufficient landing zone, is recommended in the treatment of multisegmental aneurysms of the thoracic aorta in order to facilitate the further downstream repair
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S257 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 I C - When performing the frozen and conventional elephant trunk techniques, a distal anastomosis in arch zone 2 should be considered
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S258 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 IIa C - In the case of an elephant trunk implant, the polyester trunk component should be considered to be accessible in zone
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S259 Revascularization Strategy - - reduce the risk of neurologic complications such as stroke and spinal cord ischaemia - critical (1.00) 0.54 / European Journal of Cardio-Thoracic Surgery In any TEVAR involving zone , left subclavian artery revascularization is recommended to reduce the risk of neurologic complications such as stroke and spinal cord ischaemia
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S260 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.58 I B [600] TEVAR is recommended as the firstchoice therapy for acute complicated descending aortic syndromes
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S261 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 I B [601, 602] Continued If LSA-to-LCCA bypass/transposition or double transposition cannot create a sufficient proximal landing zone, the FET technique should be considered
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S262 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.58 IIa C - If concomitant valvular or coronary disease requiring treatment is present together with acute and chronic thoracic aortic pathology involving the aortic arch, the FET technique should be considered
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S263 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 IIa B [247, 254] Stent-graft oversizing in degenerative/ atherosclerotic aneurysms or PAUs should be considered <15–20% of the proximal and distal landing zone diameters
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S264 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.58 I C - Stent-graft oversizing in acute aortic dissection/IMH should be considered <10% of the proximal landing zone diameter
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S265 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 I C - It should be considered that the landing zone diameter should not exceed 38 mm in diameter
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S266 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 IIa B [603] It should be considered that the proximal landing zone length be at least 25 mm
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S267 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 IIa C - It should be considered that the distal landing zone length be at least 25 mm
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S268 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 IIa C - In TEVAR for type B aortic dissection, distal tapering for the prevention of dSINE should be considered according to the TL diameter
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S269 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 The healthy PSZ in the descending aorta or the distal aortic arch should exceed 25 mm, whereas the DSZ should be 25 mm and 50 mm if the distal deployment is in the native aorta or a previously implanted prosthesis, respectively [128]
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S270 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.58 The treatment of chronic conditions (PAU, descending TAA and chronic type B dissections) is recommended with an oversizing of 15–20% in the proximal and distal landing zones
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S271 General Coronary Revascularization - - reduce the irritations of the aortic wall in the PSZ [622, 623] - critical (1.00) 0.62 Thus, for the acute and the subacute dissections, a moderate proximal oversizing of <10% and the use of endografts with low radial force and without proximal bare struts are recommended in order to reduce the irritations of the aortic wall in the PSZ [622, 623]
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S272 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.58 Therefore, accurate distal endograft sizing still constitutes a challenge when planning the procedure and should consider the dissection chronicity, ultrasound-based membrane elasticity, the circumference-derived true lumen diameter and the growth of the aortic diameter immediately after the onset of the dissection [409, 624]
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S273 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.58 / European Journal of Cardio-Thoracic Surgery The PETTICOAT technique should be considered in acute aortic dissections as a distal adjunct to TEVAR in case adequate true lumen decompression cannot be established by TEVAR alone
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S274 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 IIa B [604–606] The STABILISE technique may be considered in particular scenarios but preferably under controlled study conditions
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S275 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 In cases with an aorta that could not be clamped or based on the availability of specific institutional expertise, DHCA can be considered an alternative
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S276 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 In case of feasibility, a ‘distal-shifting’ strategy should have preceded any classical open TAR either by previous FET and/or TEVAR in order to achieve a proximal ‘full-fix’ [637] (Fig
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S277 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 A sequential clamping strategy is recommended [634, 635]
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S278 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 Using HCA to accomplish the proximal anastomosis should be avoided because the combination of HCA, left lung manipulation and major surgery entails risk (Fig
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S279 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 The elephant trunk or the FET component should be accessible in zone 4 [637, , 640]
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S280 General Coronary Revascularization - - reduce cardiac output, opening the aorta (in case of opening the dissection membrane), fishing for and clamping the elephant trunk/FET and stopping the rapid pacing, then Recommendation : Therapeutic options: thoracoabdominal aorta Recommendations Classa Levelb Refc For patients with low-to-moderate perioperative risk, open repair as well as endovascular treatment of pararenal and TAAA should be considered - critical (1.00) 0.54 The technical component is recommended as follows: clamping at the zone 4/5 transition, an anti-Trendelenburg position, rapid pacing to reduce cardiac output, opening the aorta (in case of opening the dissection membrane), fishing for and clamping the elephant trunk/FET and stopping the rapid pacing, then Recommendation : Therapeutic options: thoracoabdominal aorta Recommendations Classa Levelb Refc For patients with low-to-moderate perioperative risk, open repair as well as endovascular treatment of pararenal and TAAA should be considered
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S281 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 IIa C - For patients unfit for open repair, a B/ FEVAR procedure should be considered the first-line treatment
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S282 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 IIa B [631, 632] A hybrid approach may be considered for patients unfit for open repair and anatomically unsuitable for a B/ FEVAR procedure
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S283 General Coronary Revascularization - - reduce left lung manipulation to a minimum - critical (1.00) 0.54 IIb C - A ‘distal-shifting’ strategy may be considered in any TAAA to reduce left lung manipulation to a minimum
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S284 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 IIb C - For patients at high risk for SCI undergoing endovascular treatment of type I, II, III or V thoraco-abdominal aortic aneurysms, a staged TEVAR–B/FEVAR approach should be considered
