EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S285 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 IIa C - In open TAAA repair, proximal clamping before the full establishment of CPB to avoid retrograde embolization of parietal thrombi should be considered
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S286 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 IIa C - Continued In patients undergoing open descending or thoraco-abdominal aorta repair, cryoablation of multiple intercostal spaces (temporary nerve blockage) may be considered for pain control as an adjuvant strategy
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S287 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 IIb C - Left heart bypass or partial femoral– femoral bypass for open thoracoabdominal aorta repair should be considered based on surgical and institutional experience
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S288 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.58 Selective kidney protection with cold saline or Bretschneider’s solution can be considered [444]
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S289 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 Nowadays, next to the immediate short-term goal of prevention of aneurysm rupture, the long-term outcomes of each treatment strategy should be carefully taken into consideration
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S290 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.58 A patient’s and a surgeon’s decision to proceed with elective treatment should be preceded by careful preoperative assessment of concomitant disease and general health status (e.g
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S291 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 A midline transperitoneal or retroperitoneal surgical AAA access route should be based on the surgeon’s experience, preference and patient-specific aspects
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S292 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 angulation, tortuosity) is required for optimal sealing and fixation
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S293 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 In such cases, patients and surgeons should be aware of the associated increased complication and overall mortality rates [666–668]
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S294 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 Coil embolization of the aneurysm sac or the inferior mesenteric artery may be beneficial to prevent type II endoleak [671, 672]
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S295 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 I C - In patients with complicated PAU, dissection intramural haematoma, or pseudoaneurysm with suitable anatomy of the abdominal aorta, endovascular repair should be considered as the first option
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S296 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 IIa C - In patients with intact AAA with suitable anatomy for either open or EVAR, a shared decision-making process for each approach, including life expectancy, is recommended
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S297 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 I C - In patients with long life expectancy, open AAA repair should be considered the preferred treatment modality
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S298 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 IIa B [651–654] In patients with suitable anatomy and reasonable life expectancy, endovascular AAA repair should be considered the preferred treatment modality
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S299 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 IIa B [651–654] In patients with ruptured AAA undergoing endovascular treatment, a bifurcated device should be considered as preferable over an aorta-uniiliac device if anatomically suitable
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S300 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.58 In these patients, cross-clamping is required above 1 or 2 of the renal arteries with or without selective renal perfusion
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S301 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 In all patients with AAA, a proximal anastomosis should be performed as close as possible to the lowest renal artery
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S302 STEMI - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 Next to the administration of adequate intravenous antibiotic regimens to treat the aortic and aetiologic systemic infections, surgical intervention should be performed irrespective of aneurysm size [690, 691]
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S303 Revascularization Strategy - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 The sacrifice of 1 IIA may be considered if the revascularization is technically challenging (e.g
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S304 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 / European Journal of Cardio-Thoracic Surgery Recommendation : First branch vessels Recommendations Classa Levelb Refc In patients with involvement of the iliac axis in the disease process, the preservation of blood flow to at least 1 hypogastric artery is recommended
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S305 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.58 I B [695] In patients with isolated iliac artery aneurysms, elective treatment should be considered at a diameter threshold of a minimum of 35 mm
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S306 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 IIa B [696, 697] In patients with isolated iliac artery aneurysms, endovascular aneurysm repair may be considered
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S307 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 In the case of the embolization and coverage of 1 IIA, the contralateral vessel should be preserved
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S308 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.58 Proximal coiling of the IIA trunk should be preferred rather than selective embolization of the branches
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S309 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 Type I endoleaks have a high risk of rupture and should be treated at the time of diagnosis [718]
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S310 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 Type IIIa endoleaks affecting aortoiliac endografts have a sizeable complication rate and should therefore be repaired promptly
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S311 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 The Recommendation : Therapeutic options: endoleaks Recommendations Classa Levelb Refc Type I and type III endoleaks are regarded as treatment failures, and reintervention is recommended
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S312 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 I C - In the presence of a type II endoleak and aneurysm growth >_10 mm, treatment should be considered in both thoracic and abdominal pathologies
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S313 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 IIa C - In the presence of a type V endoleak (endotension without depictable endoleak on standard follow-up imaging), treatment should be considered in both thoracic and abdominal pathologies
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S314 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 Consequently, retrograde perfusion of the false lumen from the aortic branches or from the entry tears downstream from the distal endograft should not be referred to as endoleaks [128]
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S315 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 Furthermore, the reperfusion of the false lumen through the membrane rupture at the distal endograft end should be described as the dSINE and not as an endoleak [128]
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S316 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 IIa C - Continued In patients with a prosthetic vascular graft, with positive blood cultures without a clear source, multimodality imaging (CTA, 18F-FDG PET/CT) to evaluate potential graft infection is recommended
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S317 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 I B [729–731] In patients with vascular graft infection who are fit for surgery, open surgical repair is recommended
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S318 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.58 I C - In patients with vascular graft infection and new-onset pseudoaneurysm or ongoing bleeding, emergency operative management is recommended
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S319 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 I C - In patients with vascular graft infection and active bleeding into the airway or gastrointestinal tract, endovascular treatment may be considered as a bridging strategy
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S320 General Coronary Revascularization - stable - - critical (1.00) 0.58 IIb C - In patients with vascular graft infection who are haemodynamically stable without vascular discontinuity, scheduled open surgery should be considered
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S321 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 IIa C - Surgical management Surgical management of vascular graft infection, including excision of prosthetic material, local debridement and preferably orthotopic vascular reconstruction, is recommended
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S322 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 The timing of surgery for vascular graft infections in In patients with suspected vascular graft infection undergoing open surgical repair, multiple samples for obtaining conventional culture, 16S RNA genomic analysis and pathological examination are recommended
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S323 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 I C - For surgical vascular graft infection management, liberal use of adjunct therapies such as delayed cavity closure, use of coverage flaps and continuous irrigation may be considered based on individual characteristics and local expertise
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S324 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 IIb C - Postsurgical management In patients who were operated on for vascular graft infection, a postoperative antimicrobial regimen for at least 6 weeks is recommended, if the tissue samples from retrieved specimens are culture positive
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S325 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 I C - In patients with intraoperative purulence, MRSA, or infection caused by a multidrug-resistant microorganism or Candida spp, a 6-month regimen of antimicrobial therapy is recommended
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S326 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 In selected patients, life-long antibiotic therapy is recommended
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S327 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 Coxiella brunetti, Mycobacterium spp, Mycoplasma spp, Bartonella spp) extending the postoperative antimicrobial course beyond 6 weeks may be considered
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S328 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 IIb C - 18F-PET/CT should be considered to decide on the duration of prolonged antimicrobial course for vascular graft infection, particularly in patients with a retained vascular graft
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S329 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 IIa C - In patients with vascular graft infection due to a low-virulence causative agent and favourable clinical/imaging features, medical management alone and close follow-up may be considered
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S330 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 Multiple surgical specimens should be retrieved and labelled at the time of surgery for pathological examination and microbiological processing (including genomic sequencing to increase diagnostic yield)
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S331 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 The therapeutic option should be individualized according to the severity and extension of infection and the general condition of the patient
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S332 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 For such fistulas, temporary TEVAR coverage should not be utilized as a sole therapeutic strategy [754]
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S333 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 This as stated, should only be contemplated as a bailout in exceptional situations
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S334 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 In the absence of further evidence surgeons within multidisciplinary teams should apply their best treatment choice, considering their experience, patient’s age, and clinical condition
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S335 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 For large periaortic abscesses or collections, the option of preoperative percutaneous drainage to decrease the bacterial burden may be considered [759]
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S336 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 Discontinuation of antibiotic treatment should be closely monitored
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S337 General Coronary Revascularization - symptomatic - - critical (1.00) 0.54 Most commonly, the aberrant subclavian artery passes posterior to the Recommendation : Rare conditions: Kommerell diverticulum Recommendations Classa Levelb Refc In symptomatic patients with a high suspicion (dysphagia) of an aberrant subclavian artery and/or KD, CTA or MRI is recommended
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S338 General Coronary Revascularization - - improve surveillance and facilitate operative decision-making - critical (1.00) 0.54 I C - It is recommended to offer multiple measurements of the KD and the adjacent aorta to improve surveillance and facilitate operative decision-making
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S339 General Coronary Revascularization - symptomatic - - critical (1.00) 0.54 I C - In symptomatic patients with aberrant subclavian artery and/or KD, treatment is recommended
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S340 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 I C - In patients with aberrant subclavian artery and/or KD, treatment may be considered when the diverticulum orifice is >_30 mm and/or when the combined diameter of the diverticulum and the adjacent aorta (DAW) is >_50 mm and/or when the diameter of the aberrant artery is >_30 mm
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S341 General Coronary Revascularization - symptomatic - - critical (1.00) 0.54 IIb C - In patients with a symptomatic aberrant subclavian artery, transsection of the vascular ring and transposition or bypass is recommended to release compression and to re-establish arterial circulation
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S342 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 I C - In young patients without significant comorbidities, open surgical treatment (via a thoracotomy and carotid-to-subclavian artery bypass) of KD is recommended
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S343 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.58 I C - In patients with an emergency presentation or who might not tolerate open surgery, hybrid (closed-chest) repair of the KD is recommended
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S344 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 I C - In patients with KD and indication for treatment, open surgical resection and endovascular repair with subclavian debranching may be considered depending on the patient’s anatomical characteristics, age and general status
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S345 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 For consistency, multiple measurements are recommended (Fig
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S346 General Coronary Revascularization - a KD - - critical (1.00) 0.62 In patients with a KD, preventive surgery is recommended when the risk of surgery is lower than the risk of rupture and/or dissection
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S347 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 Open aortic replacement of the aorta may be considered based 63 M
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S348 General Coronary Revascularization - symptomatic (dysphagia / symptomatic - - critical (1.00) 0.62 In symptomatic patients (dysphagia, dyspnoea) operative management should guarantee full decompression of the involved surrounding structures
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S349 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 TAR via the FET procedure may be considered in the presence of concomitant aortic arch dilatation [779]
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S350 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 Endovascular approaches may be considered in patients with suitable anatomy, particularly in patients with increased operative risk
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S351 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 In young patients, given the relatively low incidence of comorbidities, open repair through a thoracotomy is preferred due to its durability and efficacy in symptom relief [774, 776]
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S352 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 Patients with CoA should undergo TTE for initial assessment [786, 787] because this modality provides functional and anatomical information regarding site, structure and extent of CoA, LV function and the presence of hypertrophy and concomitant cardiac abnormalities
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S353 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 In the case of suboptimal acoustic windows, cardiac MRI [789, 790] is recommended
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S354 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 In addition, head CTA is recommended for exclusion of intracranial aneurysms, which are often present in patients with CoA and account for 5% of deaths [792]
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S355 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 Magnetic resonance imaging techniques are a useful tool in reducing life-long radiation while providing adequate spatiotemporal resolution and can substitute CTA
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S356 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 Before the introduction of surgical treatment, the mean age of death was 34 years, with a quarter of patients dying Recommendation : Rare conditions: coarctation of the aorta Recommendations Classa Levelb Refc In patients with a diagnosis of CoA, primary or recurrent and with clinical significance, treatment is recommended
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S357 General Coronary Revascularization - symptomatic - - critical (1.00) 0.54 I C - Asymptomatic patients with a diagnosis of CoA but in whom there is a systolic gradient across the stenosis at rest (>20 mmHg) and/ or SBP difference above 10 mmHg combined with coarctation-attributed HF and/or proximal hypertension should be considered for open or endovascular repair
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S358 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 IIa C - Regardless of the pressure gradient, patients with documented HTA with >50% aortic luminal narrowing in relation to the aortic diameter at the level of the diaphragm (based on MRI, CT or invasive angiography measurements) should be considered for treatment
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S359 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 IIa C - For adult patients with a diagnosis of CoA, referral to a specialized aortic centre should be considered
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S360 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 IIa C - In patients with a diagnosis of coarctation of the native thoracic aorta as well as after previous repair and suitable anatomy, endovascular repair should be considered as an alternative to open surgical repair
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S361 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 IIa C - In patients requiring coarctation re-repair, extra anatomical bypass from the ascending to the descending aorta may be considered at the time of concomitant cardiac repair
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S362 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 Computed tomography and 18F-FDG PET are useful for the diagnosis of suspected inflammatory disease of the aorta and for the evaluation of therapy during the follow-up period, all this being widely recommended [141]
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S363 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 The indications for peripheral vascular reconstruction in patients with TA must be carefully individualized [851]
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S364 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 Considering the patterns of presentation, imaging follow-up is recommended for all patients with GCA and other inflammatory ADs [866]
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S365 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 however, one should keep in mind that in-hospital mortality is significant [881]
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S366 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 The lower rates of aneurysms, especially at the level of the abdominal aorta, should be tempered by the fact that a similar aortic diameter threshold (of 30 mm) in both genders may underestimate the actual prevalence of aneurysms in women
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S367 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 / European Journal of Cardio-Thoracic Surgery Recommendation : Aortic disease in women: pregnancy and post partum Recommendations Classa Levelb Refc Delivery in pregnant patients with aortopathy In pregnant patients with a history of aortic dissection, C-section is recommended
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S368 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 I C - In pregnant patients with an aortic diameter of <40 mm, vaginal delivery is recommended
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S369 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 I C - C-section should be considered in pregnant patients with a diameter >_45 mm of the aortic root or ascending aorta
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S370 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 IIa C - Surgery before pregnancy in women with aortic disease Aortic repair before pregnancy is recommended in patients with Marfan’s syndrome and aortic root diameter >_45 mm
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S371 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 I C - Continued With an aortic root diameter of 40–45 mm, aortic repair before pregnancy may be considered in the presence of risk factors for aortic dissection (family history or rapid growth >3 mm/year)
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S372 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.58 IIb C - Pregnancy in patients with aortopathy: dissection and surgery in pregnancy Urgent aortic repair is recommended with foetal monitoring in patients with acute type A aortic dissection during the first 2 trimesters
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S373 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.58 I C - In patients with acute type A aortic dissection during the third trimester, urgent caesarean delivery immediately followed by aortic surgery is recommended
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S374 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.58 I C - OMT is recommended in patients with acute type B aortic dissection during pregnancy unless complications develop
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S375 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 This difference has opened the debate on whether a similar diameter threshold should be used to decide intervention in women and in men [894]
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S376 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 It is recommended that women with a risk for aortopathy having a planned pregnancy undergo imaging including echocardiography, MRI or CT, to elucidate the entire vascular condition
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S377 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 Surveillance imaging throughout pregnancy and several weeks post partum using ultrasound is recommended to monitor for congenital changes in aortic size
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S378 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 In women with one of the previously mentioned hereditary conditions, or a known aortopathy, pre-pregnancy genetic counselling, baseline aortic imaging and understanding of the potential risk of dissection with the aim of shared decision-making with the patient are recommended
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S379 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 In the grey zone between 40 mm and 45 mm of root dimension, shared decision-making with a patient is required [907, 908], because recommendations are not clear: The World Health Organization classification for cardiovascular risk for women with Marfan syndrome and moderate aortic dilation is risk class III, whereas those with an aortic root diameter of >45 mm are considering to be in risk class IV [909]
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S380 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 Because of the increased risk of aortic dissection, pregnancy should be avoided when the aortic root is >45 mm and pre-emptive repair should be considered
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S381 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 Patients at risk in their families should realize a slightly enhanced risk of contracting aortic dissection, even in the post-partum period
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S382 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.58 In the planning phase of any pre-emptive aortic surgery, the risk and the potential benefit should be discussed in detail, with operations ranging from a valve-sparing root replacement operation such as a David 5 and the Yacoub procedure, to a rapid procedure involving the aorta according to the Paediatric Emergency Assessment, Recognition, and Stabilization concept
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S383 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 Aortic dissection and aortic surgery in pregnancy Usually during pregnancy, if marked aortic dilation is present or rapid aortic diameter progression >3 mm is noted, a certain risk of maternal aortic dissection should be discussed
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S384 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 decision-making process and should be individualized based on maternal and foetal risk assessment
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S385 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 A dedicated care team usually located at an AC should be involved
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S386 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.58 If type A aortic dissection occurs in the first 26 weeks of pregnancy, emergency cardiac surgery should be performed, recognizing the risk of foetal loss [388, 903]
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S387 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 In the aggregate, prophylactic aortic surgery during pregnancy requires a complex decision-making process, and management should be individualized based on maternal and foetal risk and benefits
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S388 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 ‘As low as reasonably achievable’ principles to protect patient and team members should be applied by physicians performing aortic interventions [916]
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S389 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 I C - Normothermia is recommended in the early postoperative phase
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S390 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 I C - The femoral artery should be considered as preferred for arterial pressure monitoring postoperatively
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S391 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 IIa C - Close monitoring of lactate levels as a surrogate for postoperative malperfusion is recommended
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S392 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 I C - Point-of-care monitoring, in addition to regular laboratory coagulation parameters, should be considered in the early postoperative phase after open aortic repair
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S393 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 Therefore, femoral arterial pressure monitoring should be preferred in the early postoperative phase
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S394 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 Pulmonary artery catheter or continuous cardiac output monitoring using arterial waveform analysis should be considered as first-line monitoring measures
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S395 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 Due to the interaction of temperature and the coagulation system, respectively, and the higher rate of blood loss, postoperative inadvertent hypothermia should be avoided, and patients should be kept normothermic in the postoperative phase [925]
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S396 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 In general, clinical assessment should address respiratory, cardiovascular, hepatic, coagulation, renal and neurologic systems
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S397 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 Neurologic evaluation should be facilitated as soon as possible after aortic repair by weaning the patient from sedatives and enabling spontaneous ventilation
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S398 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 Adherence to an enhanced recovery protocol after surgery should be pursued, including early weaning from sedatives and transferring the patient to an intermittent mandatory ventilation mode
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S399 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 Once the patient is admitted to the ICU, laboratory as well as point-of-care (POC) coagulation monitoring (thromboelastography) is required in order to maintain haemostasis and detect haemostatic abnormalities early
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S400 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 In accordance with the 2017 EACTS/EACTA Guidelines for Patient Blood Management, transfusion strategies should be implemented [922, 931]
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S401 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 Liberal substitution of fibrinogen should be recommended in patients with low fibrinogen levels and evidence of postoperative microvascular bleeding
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S402 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 In terms of transfusion triggers for platelet concentrates in bleeding patients, a cut-off Rescue protocols for postoperative SCI after aortic arch surgery procedures that include CSF drainage and blood pressure elevation are recommended
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S403 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 I C - Immediate CSF drainage in patients with new onset of paraplegia after TEVAR or open TAAA replacement is recommended
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S404 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 I C - For patients with ILT after FET procedures, therapeutic anticoagulation is recommended
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S405 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 I C - Early TEVAR extension may be considered in patients with ILT after a FET implant
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S406 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 Regular clinical assessments are recommended [933]
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S407 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 In cases of SCI, patients should at least be treated with CSF drainage for 5 days to allow for reorganization of the arterial collateral spinal network [949]
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S408 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 Patients treated with CSF drainage should be kept in a lying position to avoid intracranial pressure drop, possibly resulting in a subdural haematoma
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S409 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 ILT was a predictor of early mortality in 1 study and should be treated with therapeutic anticoagulation
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S410 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 Optimal medical treatment of blood pressure is recommended in all types of AD and should always be part of the postoperative management
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S411 General Coronary Revascularization - - The use of ß-blockers - critical (1.00) 0.62 The use of ß-blockers should be considered as a first-line treatment along with the implementation of calcium channel antagonists and/or renin-angiotensin inhibitors, if required [324]
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S412 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 In both patient cohorts, slightly higher systolic blood pressure (mean arterial pressure 80–90 mmHg) and haemoglobin levels of 10 mg/dl may be considered up to the exclusion of a neurologic deficit and according to the individual patient’s risk for paraplegia [958]
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S413 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 In case of paraplegia, higher blood pressure levels should be continued, and placement of a CSF drain should follow immediately after correction of the coagulopathy
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S414 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.58 Especially in acute complicated TBAD, the ICU team should be aware of the characteristics of the true lumen and perfusion of the peripheral arteries before and after the intervention in order to estimate the individual patient’s risk for organ ischaemia and to initiate specific examinations and treatment
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S415 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 Pulse deficit and peripheral perfusion should be evaluated in both legs to estimate the level of true lumen collapse or thrombosis at the iliac artery axis and more distally
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S416 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 Computed contrast enhanced tomography or invasive angiography in hybrid operating room settings should be considered in order to verify the diagnosis and to initiate immediate treatment
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S417 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 In the ICU setting, echocardiography is recommended to exclude or detect the presence of a dissection flap in the proximal aorta and pericardial effusion in case of chest pain, haemodynamic depression and stroke followed by CT angiography
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S418 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 LIVING WITH AORTIC DISEASE Recommendation : Living with aortic disease Recommendations Classa Levelb Refc Exercise and sports Thorough education regarding the individual risks and benefits of exercise is recommended
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S419 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 I C - For patients with adequately controlled BP, 30–60 min of mild-to-moderate dynamic exercise at least 3–4 days per week is recommended
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S420 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 III C - Cardiac rehabilitation In patients after invasive treatment of aortic pathologies, an individual cardiac rehabilitation programme under medical supervision is recommended
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S421 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 I C - Surveillance In any patients receiving treatment of aortic pathologies, a diseaseand treatment-based individual surveillance programme in a specialized aortic centre with a dedicated outpatient clinic is recommended
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S422 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 I C - Imaging-based quality control after every open or endovascular aortic procedure is recommended, irrespective of the treated segment before discharge
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S423 General Coronary Revascularization - stable - - critical (1.00) 0.58 I C - In patients after treatment for acute aortic dissection or IMH irrespective of treatment modality, CTA surveillance is recommended after 6months and 12months and, in case of stable conditions, annually thereafter for 5years
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S424 General Coronary Revascularization - stable - - critical (1.00) 0.58 I C - In patients after endovascular treatment irrespective of the underlying aortic disease, CTA surveillance is recommended after 6months and 12months and, in case of stable conditions, annually thereafter for 5years
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S425 General Coronary Revascularization - stable - - critical (1.00) 0.58 I C - In patients after open surgical treatment for non-aortic dissection or IMH pathologies, imaging surveillance is recommended after 12 months and 24 months and, in case of stable conditions, should be extended thereafter
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S426 General Coronary Revascularization - stable - - critical (1.00) 0.58 I C - In patients with stable aortic conditions, extended surveillance intervals should be considered after 5 years based on an individual protocol
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S427 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 IIa C - In case of non-operability, no option or informed consent of refraining from treatment, stopping surveillance is recommended
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S428 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 A patient with connective tissue disease involving the aorta or one already diagnosed with an aortic pathology should be individually advised by clinicians by expounding upon the positive and negative effects of exercise on the given AD
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S429 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 A major effect of sports is obviously the positive effect on quality of life—a factor of utmost importance, especially in patients diagnosed with inherent AD who are still burdened by stigmatization and social difficulties that should not be exacerbated by the unnecessary prohibition of physical activity
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S430 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 This issue must be discussed openly to enable shared decision-making with the patient, parents and coach(es)
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S431 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 Nevertheless, a patient with an aortic aneurysm or who has had an aortic dissection should be encouraged to engage in sports
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S432 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 Because the maximum increase in blood pressure occurs at the level when an additional repetition cannot be carried out, irrespective of the amount of weight, patients with AD should only use low weights and stop exercise well before muscle fatigue occurs [968]
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S433 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 Nevertheless, close surveillance via a low-static and low-dynamic individual programme overseen by an experienced team is recommended
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S434 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 Patients should therefore discuss returning to work with their physician or surgeon regarding time points and the need for additional surveillance
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S435 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 The CR programme should be started as early as possible and carried out under professional medical supervision in close cooperation with the primary treating AC [974]
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S436 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 Nevertheless, as a general rule, the more untreated segments there are remaining, the more frequently the patient should be seen, whereas there is a gradient from high-risk patients (dissection patients with endovascular or medical treatment) to low-risk patients (aneurysm patients after open repair) for subsequent aortic events that should be taken into account for planning an individual surveillance programme in each patient
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S437 General Coronary Revascularization - - - - critical (1.00) 0.54 Several areas have been identified where more research is needed, gaps that the global aortic community should strive to address: • Identifying diagnostic biomarkers for confirming aortic dissection

Tabla de Candidatos EO

Estado institucional y avance de auditoria de cada EO candidate derivado.

0 visibles437 totales
Statement Tipo / Subtipo Nivel / Revision Auditoria Clinica / Quirurgica Finalizacion / Fuerza Peso Texto del Statement
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S001 recommendation / general_recommendation L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) The corresponding increase in expertise needed to be able to perform these procedures and maintain quality outcomes should be considered
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S002 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) DIAGNOSTIC WORK-UP AND IMAGING 17 Recommendation : Diagnostic work-up and imaging Recommendations Levelb Clinical risk assessment Preoperative risk assessment is I B [159–162] TTE is recommended as part of the preoperative work-up
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S003 recommendation / contextual_statement L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) Thus, these software tools should be inserted into the clinical workflow, if possible, even if none are available for aortadedicated imaging, to reduce the risk of a missed aneurysm
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S004 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) In the presence of complications, such as malperfusion and rupture, urgent surgery is recommended, equal to that for type A aortic dissection
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S005 recommendation / contextual_statement L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) BAV plus other risk factors), surgery can be considered at >_50 mm even for the ascending phenotype: Factors to consider include growth rate, family history, hypertension, younger age, short stature and patient’s willingness to be operated on earlier [141, 339] (‘recommendations for root and ascending aorta’)
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S006 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) Whereas the smooth muscle cells 38 In patients with HTAD attributable to a pathogenic variant in PRKG, surgery on the aortic root or ascending aorta may be considered at a diameter of >_42 mm
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S007 recommendation / contextual_statement L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) If endovascular treatment is likely to achieve a favourable result in the presence of high-risk features, lower thresholds are reasonable
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S008 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) In patients with an incomplete circle of Willis diagnosed in preoperative coronary CTA, trilateral antegrade perfusion may be considered by additional cannulation or intubation of the LSA
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S009 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) IIa C Hybrid procedures may be considered for aortic arch repair
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S010 recommendation / contextual_statement L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) Aortic centres should represent the standard of care for acute aortic syndrome
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S011 recommendation / contextual_statement L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) However, in essence, although these recommendations serve as a valuable resource to guide clinical practice, their application should always be tailored to the needs of the individual patient
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S012 recommendation / contextual_statement L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) Furthermore, these guidelines should not be interpreted as legally binding documents
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S013 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) Is recommended/is indicated Class II Conflicting evidence and/or a divergence of opinion about the usefulness/efficacy of the given treatment or procedure
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S014 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) May be considered Class III Evidence/general agreement that the given treatment/procedure is not useful/effective and may sometimes be harmful
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S015 recommendation / general_recommendation L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) I C - The use of the TEMd classification should be considered in any acute aortic syndrome to determine the type of disease and an initial treatment strategy
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S016 recommendation / general_recommendation L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) IIa C - The use of the GERAADAe score should be considered in patients with acute type A aortic dissection undergoing surgery to determine 30-day mortality
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S017 recommendation / contextual_statement L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) This proposed wording should help create a better understanding of the pathophysiology as well as help standardize communication among physicians describing the pathological categories
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S018 recommendation / contextual_statement L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) All other procedures on the arch should be named partial arch replacement
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S019 recommendation / contextual_statement L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) These characteristics should be clearly separated from an earlier version of a non-A non-B dissection defined by an entry tear in the arch with retrograde extension, which is nothing but an overlap and subset of type A dissection [49]
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S020 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) The use of Ishimaru zones is recommended when reporting on both open and endovascular aortic treatment extension, AD extension (such as aneurysm or dissection) and location of communications between aortic lumens in patients with an aortic dissection
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S021 care_pathway / workflow_recommendation L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) Therefore, the TEM classification or the descriptive approach defining the dissection anatomy according to Ishimaru zones (for example, aortic dissection with entry in zone 0 extending down to zone ) is recommended to be used in clinical practice and in the literature
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S022 recommendation / contextual_statement L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) Most recently, a large group of international experts developed a consensus statement on nomenclature and classification of BAV and the associated aortopathy: The valve should be described as ‘fused’, ‘2-sinus’ or ‘partial-fusion’ type, whereas the aortopathy has been classified as ‘root phenotype’ (15–20% cases, dilatation prevailing at the level of the sinuses), ‘ascending phenotype’ (70–75%, dilatation prevailing at the tubular tract) or ‘extended phenotypes’ (5–10%, either root dilatation with significant extension into the tubular tract or tubular dilatation involving also the proximal arch) [80]
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S023 recommendation / contextual_statement L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) the root phenotype (or the root extended) should undergo stricter surveillance and an earlier operation
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S024 recommendation / contextual_statement L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) Not only should the intervened-upon segment be followed for healing or disease progression, pseudoaneurysm, endoleak, stent-induced new entry or antegrade/retrograde dissection, but the native aorta, whether normal or residually diseased (e.g
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S025 recommendation / contextual_statement L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) Further study is required, but, eventually, regional specialization for the treatment of AD, while maintaining adequate access to care, may optimize outcomes, especially for highmortality conditions and high-risk procedures
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S026 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) / European Journal of Cardio-Thoracic Surgery Recommendation : Aortic teams and healthcare implications Recommendations Classa Levelb Refc Shared decision-making for the optimal treatment of aortic pathologies by a multidisciplinary aortic team is recommended
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S027 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) I C - In patients with multisegmental aortic disease, treatment is recommended in aortic centres providing open and endovascular cardiac and vascular surgery on site
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S028 recommendation / general_recommendation L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) I C - Transfer to an aortic centre should be considered for patients with complex aortic pathologies
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S029 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) IIa B [105–118] For endovascular aortic procedures, a hybrid operating room, including an integrated imaging system, is recommended
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S030 recommendation / contextual_statement L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) The ultimate aim of an aortic team should be to provide a comprehensive care model designed to streamline an earlier and more accurate diagnosis and to implement appropriate timely therapy, including optimal medical therapy, open surgical intervention or an endovascular procedure and ultimately to produce optimal clinical outcomes
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S031 recommendation / contextual_statement L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) Ultimately, the aortic team should embrace a multidisciplinary approach according to the patient’s needs, specific aortic segment involved, clinical presentation, proposed therapeutic options and estimated risks according to the experience of the surgical team [137–140]
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S032 recommendation / contextual_statement L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) An aortic team should encompass a variety of expert specialists as a comprehensive service line whose knowledge and expertise are available on a 24/7 basis to treat all types of acute and chronic thoracic and abdominal aortic conditions in elective and emergency settings, using surgical and endovascular interventions
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S033 recommendation / contextual_statement L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) Although the patient with AD can be initially evaluated at every layer of the health-care system, core members of the aortic team should include cardiovascular surgeons, cardiologists and radiologists because all have relevant roles in the treatment of AD [128, , 143]
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S034 recommendation / contextual_statement L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) Moreover, for the routine practical approach to the patient and for specific clinical questions, anaesthesiologists, perfusionists, critical care specialists, geneticists, genetic counsellors, neurologists, pulmonologists, renal specialists, specialized nurses and social workers should also be considered as part of the aortic team
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S035 recommendation / contextual_statement L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) Standard operating procedures of the aortic team, including a regular meeting schedule and agenda and a regional network organized around a specific aortic centre (AC) should be defined locally [105]
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S036 recommendation / contextual_statement L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) Patients with AASs who present outside regular work hours must be assessed by the on-call specialists
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S037 recommendation / contextual_statement L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) Although there is some debate as to the optimal manner in which to regionalize care in patients with AD [134], the existence of an AC has merit and should be balanced against the need for optimal timing in the treatment of aortic pathology for maximal patient benefit [53]
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S038 recommendation / contextual_statement L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) Several characteristics have been described that should distinguish an AC from its institutional counterpart, which include providing the highest-level quality of patient care, innovation through research, education of both learners and colleagues and defining standards of care [108]
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S039 recommendation / contextual_statement L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) Distinguishing features of a comprehensive AC should encompass the following:
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S040 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) / European Journal of Cardio-Thoracic Surgery Recommendation : Diagnostic work-up and imaging Recommendations Classa Levelb Refc Clinical risk assessment Preoperative risk assessment is recommended
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S041 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) I B [159–162] TTE is recommended as part of the preoperative work-up
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S042 recommendation / general_recommendation L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) I B [163–166] Coronary evaluation/assessment should be considered to rule out coronary artery disease
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S043 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) IIa B [167] Imaging—morphology and function As the first line diagnostic modality, CTA from the common carotid to the common femoral arteries is recommended for imaging the aorta and its pathologies
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S044 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) I C - For repetitive imaging of the aorta, it is recommended that the same imaging modality be used, with a similar method of measurement to assess change in diameter
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S045 recommendation / general_recommendation L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) I C - Assessment of patency and morphology of the circle of Willis should be considered prior to total aortic arch replacement
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S046 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) IIa C - In patients with known or suspected aortic disease, it is recommended that aortic diameters be measured at reproducible anatomical landmarks (and its maximal diameter) perpendicular to the axis of blood flow
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S047 recommendation / general_recommendation L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) I C - In asymmetric or oval contour cases, reporting the longest diameter and its perpendicular diameter should be considered
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S048 recommendation / contextual_statement L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) IIa C - When performing CT or MRI, one should consider measuring the aorta from the outer edge to the outer edge, if the vessel wall is visible
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S049 recommendation / contextual_statement L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) However, beyond this clinical risk score, a constellation of first-line simple examinations, including the electrocardiogram (ECG), the chest X-ray, TTE and biomarkers, is performed primarily in patients with chest pain in the emergency room and can be useful to rule out alternative diagnoses (e.g
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S050 recommendation / contextual_statement L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) Otherwise, TOE can be performed in the operating room for type-A aortic dissection because it is useful to complete the information provided by TTE+CT
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S051 recommendation / contextual_statement L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) Of note, invasive therapeutic options should be weighted individually in each patient
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S052 recommendation / contextual_statement L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) Assessing baseline neurologic function is important for the initial risk stratification, and systemic cognitive assessment by experts should be part of the evaluation
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S053 recommendation / contextual_statement L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) A prior stroke could contribute to frailty and therefore should be added to the frailty index used to assess the patient’s ability to perform independent daily activities
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S054 recommendation / contextual_statement L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) A preoperative workup that includes pulmonary function tests may be beneficial, and blood work (including kidney and liver function tests) is necessary for patients undergoing any elective aortic procedure [175]
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S055 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) An invasive coronary angiogram is recommended in patients with known coronary artery disease, whereas non-invasive testing, such as cardiac CT, may be sufficient in selected patients without symptoms of coronary artery disease [175]
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S056 recommendation / general_recommendation L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) These variables should be considered for stratification of surgical risk, particularly in elderly patients
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S057 recommendation / general_recommendation L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) Evaluation of both is essential before One should consider measuring the aortic root from sinus to sinus, and the largest diameter measured should be considered as a reference value
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S058 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) IIa C - Assessment of the risk of radiation exposure is recommended, especially in younger adults and in those undergoing repetitive imaging
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S059 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) I C - For CT or MRI techniques involving the entire aorta, it is recommended that motion artefacts of the ascending aorta be minimized and that the entire aorta should be acquired in a single data set
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S060 recommendation / contextual_statement L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) In case of planned procedures on the aortic arch, the circle of Willis should also be imaged to assess its morphology and patency
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S061 recommendation / contextual_statement L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) The supply is based on 2 essential interrelated components that must be assessed before each procedure: (i) the 4-territory concept that describes the large extraspinal inflows, comprising the left subclavian, intercostal, lumbar and internal iliac arteries [176]
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S062 recommendation / contextual_statement L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) Although low, the radiation dose can accumulate with repeated imaging and should be prevented in young patients by using radiation-free modalities
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S063 recommendation / contextual_statement L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) As a minimum standard for imaging of the aorta, an isotropic resolution of 1 mm should be available in the CT scanner system used
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S064 recommendation / general_recommendation L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) For imaging the aortic root and the ascending aorta, ECGtriggered and/or fast acquisition techniques are recommended to minimize pulsation artefacts and increase measurement accuracy and diagnostic reliability
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S065 recommendation / contextual_statement L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) Nevertheless, these techniques should only be used if they do not negatively impact the minimal requirements (e.g
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S066 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) A contrast-enhanced arterial-phase series extending from the thoracic inlet to the level of the femoral artery is recommended in most cases
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S067 recommendation / contextual_statement L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) although the radiologist should be aware of this association while reading the CT scan, the scientific evidence is not strong enough to recommend a screening test for an intracranial aneurysm in patients with an aortic aneurysm
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S068 recommendation / contextual_statement L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) further, a portal venous-phase series can be useful to determine malperfusion of the abdominal organ
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S069 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) Also, a native CT series should be performed prior to the arterial-phase series at the first postoperative scan to reliably distinguish foreign material from contrast agent
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S070 recommendation / contextual_statement L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) Similar to CT, ECG-triggered acquisition should be chosen if the aortic root and ascending aorta require imaging
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S071 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) Therefore, MRI is often the first choice for the evaluation of congenital aortic malformations and is recommended for serial imaging in young patients or in patients in whom a radiation dose should be avoided (e.g
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S072 recommendation / contextual_statement L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) For agents with the lowest risk (like macrocyclic agents), assessment of renal function prior to MR scanning is not mandatory, and a patient should be encouraged not to refuse a clinically well-indicated enhanced MR examination to avoid a contrast agent [191]
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S073 recommendation / contextual_statement L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) It is useful in the evaluation of complications such as aortic regurgitation, left ventricular (LV) dysfunction and cardiac tamponade
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S074 recommendation / contextual_statement L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) It is useful in the longitudinal monitoring of the aortic root and ascending aortic dilatation, provided these aortic segments are well visualized
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S075 recommendation / contextual_statement L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) Intravascular ultrasound is useful in guiding the endovascular treatment of complex lesions of the thoraco-abdominal aorta because, in addition to assessing the landing zone, it can visualize aortic size, tortuosity, plaque burden, calcification, vascular branching and endovascular filling defects (e.g
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S076 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) Ultrasound is recommended for screening and monitoring of an abdominal aortic aneurysm [192], which is defined as >30 mm in diameter, using primarily an anterior–posterior diameter, and using the outer edge-to-outer edge measurement
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S077 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) Measurements should be performed perpendicular to the long axis of the aorta at specific segments, usually 8 measurements in the thoracic aorta (Fig
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S078 recommendation / contextual_statement L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) The diameter should be derived from cross-sectional images
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S079 recommendation / contextual_statement L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) In the past, it was argued that one should measure the aortic diameter from inner-edge to inner-edge as long as no wall changes were present
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S080 recommendation / contextual_statement L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) discrete thickening from atherosclerosis, IMH, aortitis or other processes), maximum diameter should be measured including the aorta wall using the outer-to-outer edge method
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S081 recommendation / general_recommendation L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) Not surprisingly, the aortic dimensions vary also across the heart cycle, which should be considered in the report and in the longitudinal analyses
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S082 recommendation / contextual_statement L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) The centreline should be parallel to the potential blood flow, in the middle of the true lumen and perpendicular to the cross-sectional measurements
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S083 recommendation / contextual_statement L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) The length of the ascending aorta illustrates the natural history of the ascending aortic aneurysm, and its measurement should be used to improve the risk stratification (‘see above’)
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S084 recommendation / contextual_statement L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) Nevertheless, the length of the ascending aorta should be part of the imaging reports (Fig
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S085 recommendation / contextual_statement L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) Improved visualization of the acquired image data and novel access to this data are required to extract the maximum information from these data and make them understandable for the benefit of the patient
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S086 recommendation / general_recommendation L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) A targeted systolic pressure of 100–120 mmHg and a heart rate of 60–80 bpm are recommended [208, 209]
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S087 recommendation / contextual_statement L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) Adequate invasive monitoring should be provided in an intermediate or ICU setting
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S088 recommendation / contextual_statement L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) Still, evaluation of each patient’s condition must weigh the risk of surgery—with all potential adjuncts necessary—against optimal medical therapy
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S089 recommendation / contextual_statement L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) Clinical assessment must not be forgotten or neglected in the current high-tech imaging climate
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S090 care_pathway / workflow_recommendation L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) A revascularization-first approach using endovascular fenestration or stenting is associated with lower in-hospital mortality and may be considered in patients with radiographic and clinical evidence of mesenteric malperfusion [229, 230]
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S091 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) In patients with circumferential detachment or complete avulsion of the coronary artery, coronary Recommendation : Acute aortic diseases: Type A aortic dissection Recommendations Classa Levelb Refc Initiation of emergency surgery is recommended in patients presenting with acute type A aortic dissection
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S092 care_pathway / workflow_recommendation L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) I B [211, 212] A tear-oriented approach with exclusion or resection of the primary entry tear in the ascending aorta and arch is recommended
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S093 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) I B [213, 214] Inspection and coverage of communications between lumina in the proximal descending aorta may be considered in specialized centres for prognostic reasons
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S094 recommendation / general_recommendation L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) IIb C - Despite preoperative neurologic dysfunction or non-haemorrhagic stroke, open repair should be considered
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S095 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) IIa B [215–217] In case of clinical and imaging evidence of visceral malperfusion, revascularization may be considered prior to aortic repair
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S096 recommendation / general_recommendation L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) IIb C - Antegrade systemic perfusion via axillary or direct aortic cannulation should be considered
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S097 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) IIa B [218, 219] An open distal anastomosis during lower body hypothermic circulatory arrest is recommended
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S098 recommendation / general_recommendation L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) In case of paraplegia due to spinal cord ischaemia, cerebrospinal fluid (CSF) drainage should be considered as the patient is prepared for ascending aortic replacement
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S099 recommendation / contextual_statement L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) due to the unpredictable behaviour of the dissected aorta (in particular in the vicinity of the aortic valve, coronary arteries and supra-aortic branches), swift repair is warranted, considering a mortality risk of .5% per hour while waiting for surgery [223]
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S100 recommendation / contextual_statement L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) In patients undergoing surgical aortic repair, the axillary artery should be used for arterial inflow
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S101 care_pathway / workflow_recommendation L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) In addition, a tear-oriented approach, consisting of resection or exclusion of the primary entry tear, is recommended [213, , 221]
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S102 recommendation / general_recommendation L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) Inspection of the proximal descending aorta during hypothermic circulatory arrest should be considered
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S103 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) In case of entry tears in the distal arch or proximal descending aorta, implantation of an FET may be considered [241]
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S104 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) As an alternative, post-surgical TEVAR may be considered for entry tear coverage and optimization of true lumen perfusion [136]
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S105 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) IIa C - In patients with anatomical feasibility to cover the primary entry tear, a stent graft implantation may be considered
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S106 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) / European Journal of Cardio-Thoracic Surgery Recommendation : Acute aortic diseases: type B aortic dissection Recommendations Classa Levelb Refc In patients with complicated acute type B aortic dissection and suitable anatomy, TEVAR is recommended
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S107 recommendation / general_recommendation L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) I B [249–253] In patients with acute complicated type B aortic dissection with unsuitable anatomy for TEVAR, FET repair should be considered
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S108 recommendation / general_recommendation L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) IIa B [254, 255] In acute type B aortic dissection with high-risk features, TEVAR should be considered in the subacute phase
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S109 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) IIa C - In patients with acute type B aortic dissection without high-risk features, optimal medical therapy, close monitoring and follow-up is recommended for emerging high-risk features
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S110 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) This strategy may be considered for patients with a large ascending aorta or aortic arch, retrograde extension of a type B dissection and lack of an adequate landing zone for an endovascular intervention and for patients with connective tissue disorders [247, , , 280]
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S111 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) I B [283, 287–290] Optimal medical therapies and serial imaging may be considered in patients with type A IMH in the absence of high-risk features
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S112 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) IIb C - In selected patients with acute type A IMH without high-risk features but a tear in the descending aorta, TEVAR may be considered in addition to OMT in specialized centres
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S113 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) In patients with acute complicated type B IMH, urgent aortic repair is indicated
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S114 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) In patients with unsuitable anatomy, open surgery should be performed [305]
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S115 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) In the absence of complications, medical management is recommended in the initial phase
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S116 recommendation / contextual_statement L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) Open descending aortic replacement should also be regarded as an alternative for the treatment of type B IMH, especially in cases of vasa vasorum rupture because pain relief in this setting is debatable after TEVAR
