EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S117 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) / European Journal of Cardio-Thoracic Surgery : High-risk features in intramural haematomas Age >70 years [293, 294] Initial aortic diameter >45 mm [293, 295] Mean aortic diameter growth rate >_5 mm/year [296] Wall thickness of involved segment >_10 mm [297] Pleural effusion based on Hounsfield units [298, 299] Presence of aortic ulcer or ulcer-like projection [294, 300] Recommendation : Acute aortic diseases: type B intramural haematoma Recommendations Classa Levelb Refc In patients with acute complicated type B IMH, urgent aortic repair is recommended
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S118 recommendation / general_recommendation L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) I B [305] In patients with acute type B IMH with highrisk features, TEVAR should be considered if the patient is anatomically suitable
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S119 recommendation / general_recommendation L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) IIa C - In patients with acute type B IMH without high-risk features, OMT and serial imaging are recommended
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S120 recommendation / general_recommendation L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) I B [305, 306] In patients with acute type B IMH without high-risk features, OMT, close monitoring and follow-up are recommended
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S121 recommendation / general_recommendation L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) I B [289] In patients with high-risk PAUs located in the distal arch or descending aorta, TEVAR should be considered if anatomically suitable
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S122 recommendation / general_recommendation L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) IIa B [310] In patients with high-risk PAUs located in the distal arch or descending aorta unsuitable for TEVAR, open surgical repair should be considered after careful evaluation of operative risk
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S123 recommendation / contextual_statement L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) Behind this clinical background, procedural risk of endovascular or open surgery should be weighed against frailty, comorbidities and life expectancy in this patient cohort
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S124 recommendation / contextual_statement L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) Therefore, symptomatic PAUs or PAUs with high-risk features located in the ascending aorta should undergo urgent surgical repair
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S125 recommendation / contextual_statement L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) In addition, high-risk features for malignant courses of any PAU should be evaluated () [305]
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S126 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) In high-risk PAUs distal to zone , aortic repair should be performed
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S127 recommendation / contextual_statement L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) BTAI involving the ascending aorta or the aortic arch is rare and should be treated based on individual decision-making of the aortic team
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S128 recommendation / contextual_statement L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) Therefore, physicians should have a high index of suspicion based on the preclinical situation and the type of traumatic event
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S129 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) Thoracic endovascular aortic repair is the first choice if treatment is indicated and in the presence of suitable anatomy [141]
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S130 recommendation / general_recommendation L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) For grade II BTAI lesions that present with high-risk imaging features, TEVAR should be considered the treatment strategy of choice
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S131 recommendation / contextual_statement L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) Grade III–IV lesions should be managed operatively with TEVAR
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S132 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) / European Journal of Cardio-Thoracic Surgery Recommendation : Acute aortic diseases: blunt traumatic aortic injury Recommendations Classa Levelb Refc In patients with BTAI grade I, nonoperative management, including close follow-up imaging, is recommended
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S133 recommendation / general_recommendation L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) I C - In patients with BTAI grade II and high-risk imaging features, TEVAR should be considered
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S134 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) IIa C - In patients with BTAI grade II without highrisk imaging features, nonoperative management and close follow-up imaging may be considered
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S135 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) IIb C - In patients with BTAI grades III–IV and suitable anatomy, TEVAR is recommended
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S136 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) I B [333, 334] Surgery for BAV-related aortopathy with ‘root phenotype’ is recommended when the maximum aortic diameter is 50 mm
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S137 recommendation / general_recommendation L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) I B [82, 84–87, 335] Surgery for TAV-associated aneurysms with ‘root phenotype’ should be considered when the maximum aortic diameter is 50 mm in a low-surgical-risk setting.d IIa B [87, 336–338] In patients with low surgical riske and ‘ascending phenotype’ dilatation, both with TAV and BAV, surgical treatment should be considered when the maximum aortic diameter is >52 mm
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S138 recommendation / general_recommendation L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) IIa C - Surgery for aortic dilatation in patients undergoing aortic valve surgery should be considered at a root or ascending diameter >_45 mm
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S139 recommendation / general_recommendation L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) IIa C - Surgery for aortic root dilatation in patients undergoing surgery for ascending aortic aneurysm should be considered at an aortic root diameter 45 mm
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S140 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) IIa C - In patients with young age, a family history of the acute type A aortic dissection or known HTAD who are undergoing ascending replacement, a lower threshold than 45 mm may be considered for concomitant root replacement on an individual basis
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S141 recommendation / contextual_statement L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) To define the optimal timing for an operation in terms of diameter, the risk of complications in the natural history must be weighed against the operative risk: In this respect, important advancements in knowledge, through large observational studies, have taken place in the last decade
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S142 recommendation / contextual_statement L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) It has been emphasized that real rapid growth, as commonly defined, is rarely observed: Measurement errors must be ruled out before indicating earlier surgery based solely on this criterion [14, 348]
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S143 recommendation / contextual_statement L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) Despite established diameter criteria, studies [350] demonstrated that the majority of acute aortic dissections occur at a diameter <55 mm, suggesting that Laplace’s law probably applies to the risk of rupture but not to the risk of dissection: Different forces must evidently lead to the 2 different modes of aortic wall acute mechanical failure, transmural rupture versus intimal delamination [351, 352]
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S144 recommendation / contextual_statement L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) As an ascending aorta dilates and/or elongates, it must change its radius of curvature, assuming a C-shape, because the upper and lower ends are fixed
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S145 recommendation / general_recommendation L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) Therefore, in therapeutic planning, the diameter should be considered in the broad context of patient-specific factors including aortic length and angulation, age, sex, family history, genetic variants, and so forth, but also, importantly, aortic pain, i.e
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S146 recommendation / general_recommendation L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) / European Journal of Cardio-Thoracic Surgery Recommendation : Chronic aortic diseases: maximum diameter and maximum length Recommendations Classa Levelb Refc Surgery should be considered for a symptomatic aneurysm of the ascending aorta, almost independently of size (once nonaortic causes have been eliminated)
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S147 recommendation / general_recommendation L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) IIa C - For individuals of small body size, height nomograms should be considered in the decision about indications for surgery at various aortic dimensions
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S148 recommendation / general_recommendation L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) IIa B [340] An ascending aortic length exceeding 110 mm should be considered as a risk factor for aortic events when indicating elective surgery for aortic aneurysms
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S149 recommendation / general_recommendation L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) IIa B [196] Despite metrics and precision criteria, the benefits and risks of surgical intervention versus nonoperative management should be considered on a case-by-case basis
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S150 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) IIa C - Other non-diameter criteria that also bear on the risk of aortic events may be considered in the decision-making
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S151 recommendation / general_recommendation L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) aortic repair should be considered
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S152 recommendation / general_recommendation L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) In patients with arch pathologies and diseased aortic segments distal to zone , FET repair should be considered
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S153 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) IIa B [311, 356–358] In patients with an indication for aortic repair unsuitable for open surgery, hybrid or endovascular aortic repair may be considered
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S154 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) IIb C - Hybrid or endovascular aortic repair is recommended to be performed in experienced centres with an adequate volume of both open and endovascular aortic repairs
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S155 recommendation / contextual_statement L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) Larger aortic diameters at the distal landing zone are unfavourable for successful endovascular treatment, and, given current device sizes, the distal descending thoracic aorta should be 40 mm or less to be able to treat the patient with 10–20% oversizing
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S156 recommendation / contextual_statement L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) Although the maximum aortic diameter in the thoracic aorta is usually closely monitored until it has reached the threshold for intervention, attention should also be paid to the total aortic diameter at the coeliac artery, which may already have reached 40 mm or greater before the maximum aortic diameter has reached 55 mm
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S157 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) Heritable thoracic aortic disease Recommendation : Heritable thoracic aortic disease Recommendations Classa Levelb Refc Genetic testing is recommended in patients with thoracic aortic disease <60 years of age, family history of TAD, arterial aneurysms in other segments and those with syndromic features
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S158 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) I B [169, , 373] Testing of family members is recommended by simpler, more cost-efficient Sanger sequencing of only the suspect genetic area
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S159 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) I C - Marfan syndrome In patients with Marfan syndrome, surgery on the aortic root or ascending aorta is recommended at a diameter of >_50mm
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S160 recommendation / general_recommendation L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) I B [374, 375] In patients with Marfan syndrome and high-risk features,* surgery on the aortic root or ascending aorta should be considered at a diameter of >_45 mm
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S161 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) IIa B [374, 375] In patients with Marfan syndrome without high-risk features with a high likelihood of undergoing valve-sparing aortic root replacement and very low surgical risk, surgery on the aortic root or ascending aorta may be considered at a diameter of >_45 mm when performed by an experienced aortic team
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S162 recommendation / general_recommendation L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) IIb C - In patients with Marfan syndrome, surgery of the aortic arch, descending thoracic aorta or abdominal aorta should be considered at a diameter of >_50 mm of the respective aortic segment
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S163 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) / European Journal of Cardio-Thoracic Surgery Recommendation : Chronic aortic diseases: chronic aortic dissection Recommendations Classa Levelb Refc Intervention is recommended in patients with chronic aortic dissection at a maximum aortic diameter of >_55 mm without involvement of the ascending aorta
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S164 recommendation / general_recommendation L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) I B [362, 363] In patients with HTAD with chronic aortic dissection, intervention at diameters <55 mm should be considered if the multidisciplinary aortic team makes the decision depending on the genotype, growth rate, family history and other individual patient risk factors
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S165 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) IIa C - In patients with dSINE, treatment is recommended to prevent diameter progression
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S166 recommendation / general_recommendation L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) I C - Intervention at >50 mm should be considered in patients with chronic aortic dissection if the treatment includes a multistep procedure, such as arch replacement with FET followed by TEVAR
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S167 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) / European Journal of Cardio-Thoracic Surgery Loeys–Dietz syndrome In patients with Loeys–Dietz syndrome, indication for surgery is recommended based on the specific genetic variant, aortic diameter, aortic growth rate, family history, history of aortic events, patient age and other individual patient-related factors and discussed by a multidisciplinary aortic team
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S168 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) I C - In patients with Loeys–Dietz syndrome attributable to a pathogenic variant in TGFBR1 or TGFBR, surgery on the aortic root or ascending aorta is recommended at a diameter of >_45 mm
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S169 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) I C - In patients with Loeys–Dietz syndrome attributable to a pathogenic variant in TGFBR1 and high-risk features,* surgery on the aortic root or ascending aorta may be considered at a diameter of >_40 mm
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S170 recommendation / general_recommendation L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) IIb C - In patients with Loeys–Dietz syndrome attributable to a pathogenic variant in TGFBR2 and high-risk features,# surgery on the aortic root or ascending aorta should be considered at a diameter of >_40 mm
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S171 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) IIa C - In patients with Loeys–Dietz syndrome attributable to a pathogenic variant in TGFB, surgery on the aortic root or ascending aorta may be considered at a diameter of >_50 mm
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S172 recommendation / general_recommendation L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) IIb C - In patients with Loeys–Dietz syndrome attributable to a pathogenic variant in SMAD, surgery on the aortic root or ascending aorta should be considered at a diameter of >_45 mm
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S173 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) IIa C - In patients with Loeys–Dietz syndrome attributable to a pathogenic variant in TGFBR, TGFBR2 or SMAD, surgery to replace the intact aortic arch, descending aorta or abdominal aorta at a diameter of >_45 mm may be considered
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S174 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) IIb C - In patients with Loeys–Dietz syndrome attributable to a pathogenic variant in SMAD2 or TGFB, surgery on the aortic root or ascending aorta may be considered at a diameter of >_45 mm
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S175 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) IIa C - In patients with HTAD attributable to a pathogenic variant in ACTA2 and high-risk features,* surgery on the aortic root or ascending aorta may be considered at a diameter of >_42 mm
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S176 recommendation / contextual_statement L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) Indication for surgery should be based on careful review of the individual patient by the multidisciplinary aortic team
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S177 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) / European Journal of Cardio-Thoracic Surgery In patients with HTAD attributable to a pathogenic variant in PRKG, surgery on the aortic root or ascending aorta may be considered at a diameter of >_42 mm
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S178 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) IIb C - In patients with HTAD attributable to a pathogenic variant in PRKG1 and high-risk features,* surgery on the aortic root or ascending aorta may be considered at a diameter of >_40 mm
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S179 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) IIb C - In patients with HTAD attributable to a pathogenic variant in MYLK or MYH, surgery on the aortic root or ascending aorta may be considered at a diameter of >_45 mm
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S180 recommendation / general_recommendation L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) I C - In patients with asymptomatic descending aortic aneurysms with high-risk features, repair should be considered in diameters <55mm
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S181 recommendation / general_recommendation L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) I C - In patients with asymptomatic thoracoabdominal aneurysms with high-risk features, aortic repair should be considered with diameters <55 mm
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S182 recommendation / contextual_statement L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) Hence, an open surgical or endovascular treatment should be undertaken when the risk of intervention is lower than the risk of rupture, which would cause death when untreated
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S183 recommendation / contextual_statement L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) Before reaching the threshold for surgical intervention to prevent the risk of rupture, medical therapy should be adopted to reduce aneurysm growth (e.g
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S184 recommendation / contextual_statement L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) However, in patients with high-risk AAA, earlier intervention may be reasonable [426]
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S185 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) In patients with symptomatic, unruptured AAA, urgent repair is indicated to reduce the risk of rupture [192, 412–417, , 426]
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S186 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) In patients with ruptured AAA, emergency surgical or endovascular treatment is indicated [192, 410– , , 426]
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S187 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) Because of the elective and preventive nature of any AAA intervention, stringent prior diagnosis of any comorbidities is recommended to determine the risk of intervention
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S188 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) Aortic Recommendation : Chronic aortic diseases: abdominal aorta Recommendations Classa Levelb Refc In patients with ruptured AAA, emergency surgical or endovascular treatment is recommended
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S189 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) I C - In patients with symptomatic, unruptured AAA, emergency repair is recommended, irrespective of diameter
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S190 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) I B [410, 411] In patients with asymptomatic AAA, repair is recommended when the AAA exceeds 55 mm
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S191 recommendation / general_recommendation L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) I A [192, 412–420] In women with asymptomatic AAA, repair should be considered when the AAA exceeds 50 mm
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S192 recommendation / general_recommendation L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) IIa C - AAA repair should be considered if aneurysm growth exceeds 10 mm/ year
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S193 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) IIa C - In AAA patients with high-risk features, earlier intervention at lower diameters may be considered
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S194 recommendation / general_recommendation L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) IIb C - In asymptomatic patients with AAA below the threshold for intervention, lifestyle modification, OMT and continuous surveillance are recommended
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S195 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) I C - In elective patients undergoing AAA repair, preoperative cardiac evaluation is recommended
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S196 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) I C - In elective patients undergoing AAA repair, preoperative cardiac echocardiography and/or screening for CAD (coronary artery angiography or coronary CTA) may be considered
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S197 recommendation / general_recommendation L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) Surgical results, usually with an aorto-bifemoral Y prosthesis implant, are excellent and should be considered as therapy of first choice in patients with extensive disease patterns such as TASC (Trans-Atlantic Inter-Society Consensus Document on Management of Peripheral Arterial Disease) C and D lesions [430]
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S198 recommendation / general_recommendation L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) / European Journal of Cardio-Thoracic Surgery Recommendation : How to monitor end-organ function and how to avoid end-organ injury Recommendations Classa Levelb Refc Perfusion In patients undergoing aortic surgery with involvement of the aortic arch, the preference for antegrade arterial inflow cannulation as compared to retrograde inflow cannulation should be considered
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S199 recommendation / general_recommendation L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) IIa B [218, , 432–435] Axillary artery cannulation should be considered for an antegrade inflow cannulation site for patients undergoing aortic surgery with involvement of the aortic arch
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S200 recommendation / general_recommendation L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) IIa B [218, , 432–435] Continued Multiple arterial pressure monitoring lines (upper and lower body) are recommended for aortic arch and/or thoraco-abdominal aortic repair
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S201 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) A femoral arterial line is recommended for lower body perfusion-pressure monitoring
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S202 recommendation / general_recommendation L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) I C - Temperature management Nasopharyngeal and core temperature (rectal/bladder) measurements are recommended in patients undergoing aortic arch surgery in lower body HCA
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S203 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) I C - Considering the core temperature (rectal/bladder) as the reference value for the determination of the level of lower body HCA is recommended
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S204 recommendation / contextual_statement L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) I C - A target hypothermic circulatory arrest temperature should be determined based on the anticipated extent of repair, expected duration of lower body HCA and presence of preoperative malperfusion
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S205 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) IIa C - The use of the term ‘mild hypothermia’ is recommended for core temperatures >28
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S206 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) I C - The use of the term ‘high moderate hypothermia’ is recommended for core temperatures between .1 C and
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S207 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) I C - The use of the term ‘low moderate hypothermia’ is recommended for core temperatures between .1 C and
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S208 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) I C - The use of the term ‘deep hypothermia’ is recommended for core temperatures <_20
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S209 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) I C - High-moderate lower body HCA in combination with cerebral perfusion for hemiarch replacement is recommended
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S210 recommendation / general_recommendation L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) I C - High-moderate lower body HCA in combination with selective antegrade cerebral perfusion for complex aortic arch procedures should be considered
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S211 recommendation / contextual_statement L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) IIa B [178] Target lower body HCA temperature, as well as the cerebral protection method, should be determined based on the anticipated extent of repair, expected duration of lower body HCA and the presence of preoperative malperfusion
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S212 recommendation / general_recommendation L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) IIa C - Organ protection—heart Retrograde cardioplegia should be considered to facilitate repetitive cardioplegia applications without interrupting the procedure
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S213 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) IIa C - Non-cardioplegic myocardial perfusion may be considered during extended aortic arch repair to reduce myocardial ischaemia time
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S214 care_pathway / workflow_recommendation L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) Some centres also use the carotid artery for arterial inflow, Organ protection—brain During surgery for aortic arch repair, the use of bilateral NIRS-based cerebral oximetry combined with an algorithmic approach to intervention for cortical Hb-desaturation is recommended
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S215 recommendation / general_recommendation L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) I C - In a patient with an incomplete circle of Willis diagnosed in preoperative cerebral CTA, trilateral antegrade perfusion should be considered via additional cannulation or intubation of the left subclavian artery
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S216 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) IIa C - Organ protection—spinal cord CSF drainage for spinal cord protection is recommended in open TAAA replacement
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S217 recommendation / general_recommendation L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) I B [436, 437] CSF drainage should be considered in total aortic arch replacement in patients at risk of SCI
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S218 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) IIa C - CSF drainage may be considered in type A aortic dissection patients with preoperative symptomatic SCI
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S219 recommendation / general_recommendation L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) III C - For patients at an increased risk of spinal cord injury undergoing endovascular treatment of thoracic or thoraco-abdominal aortic disease, prophylactic CSF drainage should be considered
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S220 recommendation / general_recommendation L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) IIa C - The 4-territory concept should be considered during planning to reduce procedure-induced deprivation of antegrade arterial spinal cord supply to a minimum
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S221 recommendation / general_recommendation L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) IIa C - A critical appraisal of the contribution of thoracic and lumbar segmental arteries to spinal cord perfusion should be considered before surgery to determine the need for reimplantation
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S222 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) IIa C - Prevention of steal in particular from segmental arteries to avoid SCI is recommended
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S223 recommendation / general_recommendation L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) I C - Organ protection—visceral and renal In patients undergoing aortic arch surgery repair, early reinstitution of distal aortic perfusion should be considered to reduce postoperative visceral, renal and spinal ischaemia
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S224 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) IIa B [438–441] Selective visceral and renal perfusion is recommended in open TAAA repair I B [442–445] In TAAA surgery, maintenance of lower limb perfusion to avoid rhabdomyolysis and AKI is recommended
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S225 recommendation / general_recommendation L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) I C - Deep HCA should be considered in patients undergoing open thoracoabdominal aorta repair based on surgical and institutional experience
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S226 recommendation / general_recommendation L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) / European Journal of Cardio-Thoracic Surgery A period of hypothermic perfusion at the desired target core temperature before hypothermic lower body circulatory arrest should be considered to ensure appropriate thermal distribution throughout the body
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S227 recommendation / general_recommendation L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) IIa C - A period of hypothermic perfusion before initiation of the rewarming phase should be considered to decrease end-organ injury
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S228 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) IIa C - Arterial CPB outlet temperature of <37 C during rewarming to avoid hyperthermic perfusion is recommended
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S229 recommendation / contextual_statement L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) A standardized approach to temperature measurement and reporting in future studies is warranted to allow for more accurate comparisons between studies
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S230 recommendation / contextual_statement L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) However, it must be critically questioned whether 43 M
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S231 recommendation / contextual_statement L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) In general anaesthesia, the following 3-pillared lung-protective ventilation strategy should be applied: (i) moderate hyperoxia (FiO2 not exceeding 80%)
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S232 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) Retrograde flushing may be considered to clear any debris from the carotid arteries
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S233 recommendation / contextual_statement L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) In patients undergoing hemiarch replacement or other arch intervention with circulatory arrest times <40 min, both unilateral and bilateral antegrade perfusion may be reasonable
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S234 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) In patients undergoing longer circulatory arrest periods (>40 min), bilateral cerebral perfusion from the beginning is reasonable [496–499]
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S235 recommendation / general_recommendation L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) Nevertheless, it should be considered in high-risk patients with an already reduced spinal cord blood supply (previous TEVAR/ TAR), occluded LSA or internal iliac arteries
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S236 recommendation / contextual_statement L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) Satisfactory oxygen delivery and perfusion pressure should be maintained intraoperatively (before, during and after CPB) and postoperatively to avoid end-organ injury in aortic surgery
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S237 recommendation / contextual_statement L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) During CPB it is imperative to maintain a satisfactory goal-directed perfusion pressure, always ensuring appropriate pump flow and satisfactory oxygen content (CaO), and these goals should be pursued postoperatively
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S238 recommendation / general_recommendation L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) Avoidance of rapid rewarming and hyperthermic perfusion are recommended
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S239 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) To attenuate the rewarming insult, it is recommended to avoid temperature differences between the arterial and venous lines above 3 C, with a rewarming rate no faster than 1 C every 5 min
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S240 recommendation / contextual_statement L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) Leg ischaemia leading to rhabdomyolysis in TAAA surgery is associated with AKI and should be avoided, when possible, through lower limb perfusion 46 M
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S241 recommendation / contextual_statement L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) Ischaemic markers such as lactate level and lactate/pyruvate ratio should be monitored intraoperatively
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S242 recommendation / general_recommendation L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) In these cases, preemptive protective measures should be considered in order to reduce the risk for end-organ complications
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S243 recommendation / contextual_statement L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) In addition, the target activated clotting time should be achieved and monitored throughout the procedure
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S244 recommendation / contextual_statement L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) Early introducer sheath removal or downsizing with suture-mediated closure devices or purse-string sutures should be done, when possible, to increase peripheral blood flow [557]
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S245 recommendation / contextual_statement L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) In ATAAD, the general principle of surgical treatment is to resect the segment containing the entry tear: The root must be replaced when it is either the site of the intimal tears or significantly involved by dilatation
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S246 recommendation / contextual_statement L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) root dimensions) should prompt concomitant root replacement in ATAAD without a tear in the sinuses
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S247 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) / European Journal of Cardio-Thoracic Surgery Recommendation : Therapeutic options: aortic root Recommendations Classa Levelb Refc For aortic dilatations and aneurysms involving the aortic root with a structurally diseased aortic valve, replacement of the aortic valve and sinuses with coronary ostia direct reimplantation (modified Bentall operation) is recommended
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S248 recommendation / general_recommendation L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) I B [559–561] Valve-sparing root replacement should be considered for patients with a non-diseased tricuspid aortic valve and dilated root, especially young patients, if performed by experienced surgeons
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S249 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) IIa B [562, 563] Valve-sparing root replacement may be considered for patients with a nondiseased bicuspid aortic valve and dilated root if performed by surgeons with specific expertise in aortic valve repair
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S250 recommendation / contextual_statement L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) At least in patients with HTAD, the remodelling technique without annuloplasty should not be preferred because of concerns about late postoperative annular enlargement and recurrence/occurrence of aortic insufficiency [568, 569]
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S251 recommendation / contextual_statement L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) In patients undergoing surgery for an aneurysm of the midascending aorta, a non-dilated or just mildly dilated (<_45 mm) root (‘ascending phenotype dilatation’) should not be replaced, unless there are concerns about the patient’s young age, family history of ATAAD or known HTAD
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S252 recommendation / contextual_statement L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) Moreover, ascending grafts should be chosen, anticipating further distal endovascular treatment: first, straight implantation of the prosthesis and second, a prosthesis length of >_7 cm
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S253 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) Aortic arch Recommendation : Ascending aorta with or without proximal arch Recommendations Classa Levelb Refc In ascending aortic surgery, supracommissural isolated ascending aorta replacement is recommended in patients without adjunct aortic root and aortic arch dilatation
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S254 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) I B [571, , 574] It is recommended that in any open proximal thoracic aortic operation, ascending/hemiarch replacement has to be extensive, and short ascending grafts should be avoided to prevent disease progression and to anticipate a future endovascular modular distal extension
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S255 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) I C - In ascending aortic surgery with a diameter larger than 45 mm of the proximal aortic arch, hemiarch or more extensive aortic arch replacement may be considered
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S256 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) IIa B [576–581] Aortic arch replacement, including the creation of a sufficient landing zone, is recommended in the treatment of multisegmental aneurysms of the thoracic aorta in order to facilitate the further downstream repair
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S257 recommendation / general_recommendation L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) I C - When performing the frozen and conventional elephant trunk techniques, a distal anastomosis in arch zone 2 should be considered
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S258 recommendation / general_recommendation L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) IIa C - In the case of an elephant trunk implant, the polyester trunk component should be considered to be accessible in zone
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S259 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) / European Journal of Cardio-Thoracic Surgery In any TEVAR involving zone , left subclavian artery revascularization is recommended to reduce the risk of neurologic complications such as stroke and spinal cord ischaemia
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S260 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) I B [600] TEVAR is recommended as the firstchoice therapy for acute complicated descending aortic syndromes
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S261 recommendation / general_recommendation L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) I B [601, 602] Continued If LSA-to-LCCA bypass/transposition or double transposition cannot create a sufficient proximal landing zone, the FET technique should be considered
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S262 recommendation / general_recommendation L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) IIa C - If concomitant valvular or coronary disease requiring treatment is present together with acute and chronic thoracic aortic pathology involving the aortic arch, the FET technique should be considered
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S263 recommendation / general_recommendation L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) IIa B [247, 254] Stent-graft oversizing in degenerative/ atherosclerotic aneurysms or PAUs should be considered <15–20% of the proximal and distal landing zone diameters
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S264 recommendation / general_recommendation L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) I C - Stent-graft oversizing in acute aortic dissection/IMH should be considered <10% of the proximal landing zone diameter
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S265 recommendation / general_recommendation L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) I C - It should be considered that the landing zone diameter should not exceed 38 mm in diameter
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S266 recommendation / general_recommendation L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) IIa B [603] It should be considered that the proximal landing zone length be at least 25 mm
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S267 recommendation / general_recommendation L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) IIa C - It should be considered that the distal landing zone length be at least 25 mm
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S268 recommendation / general_recommendation L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) IIa C - In TEVAR for type B aortic dissection, distal tapering for the prevention of dSINE should be considered according to the TL diameter
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S269 recommendation / contextual_statement L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) The healthy PSZ in the descending aorta or the distal aortic arch should exceed 25 mm, whereas the DSZ should be 25 mm and 50 mm if the distal deployment is in the native aorta or a previously implanted prosthesis, respectively [128]
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S270 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) The treatment of chronic conditions (PAU, descending TAA and chronic type B dissections) is recommended with an oversizing of 15–20% in the proximal and distal landing zones
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S271 recommendation / general_recommendation L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) Thus, for the acute and the subacute dissections, a moderate proximal oversizing of <10% and the use of endografts with low radial force and without proximal bare struts are recommended in order to reduce the irritations of the aortic wall in the PSZ [622, 623]
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S272 recommendation / contextual_statement L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) Therefore, accurate distal endograft sizing still constitutes a challenge when planning the procedure and should consider the dissection chronicity, ultrasound-based membrane elasticity, the circumference-derived true lumen diameter and the growth of the aortic diameter immediately after the onset of the dissection [409, 624]
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S273 recommendation / general_recommendation L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) / European Journal of Cardio-Thoracic Surgery The PETTICOAT technique should be considered in acute aortic dissections as a distal adjunct to TEVAR in case adequate true lumen decompression cannot be established by TEVAR alone
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S274 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) IIa B [604–606] The STABILISE technique may be considered in particular scenarios but preferably under controlled study conditions
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S275 recommendation / contextual_statement L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) In cases with an aorta that could not be clamped or based on the availability of specific institutional expertise, DHCA can be considered an alternative
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S276 recommendation / contextual_statement L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) In case of feasibility, a ‘distal-shifting’ strategy should have preceded any classical open TAR either by previous FET and/or TEVAR in order to achieve a proximal ‘full-fix’ [637] (Fig
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S277 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) A sequential clamping strategy is recommended [634, 635]
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S278 recommendation / contextual_statement L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) Using HCA to accomplish the proximal anastomosis should be avoided because the combination of HCA, left lung manipulation and major surgery entails risk (Fig
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S279 recommendation / contextual_statement L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) The elephant trunk or the FET component should be accessible in zone 4 [637, , 640]
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S280 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) The technical component is recommended as follows: clamping at the zone 4/5 transition, an anti-Trendelenburg position, rapid pacing to reduce cardiac output, opening the aorta (in case of opening the dissection membrane), fishing for and clamping the elephant trunk/FET and stopping the rapid pacing, then Recommendation : Therapeutic options: thoracoabdominal aorta Recommendations Classa Levelb Refc For patients with low-to-moderate perioperative risk, open repair as well as endovascular treatment of pararenal and TAAA should be considered
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S281 recommendation / general_recommendation L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) IIa C - For patients unfit for open repair, a B/ FEVAR procedure should be considered the first-line treatment
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S282 care_pathway / workflow_recommendation L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) IIa B [631, 632] A hybrid approach may be considered for patients unfit for open repair and anatomically unsuitable for a B/ FEVAR procedure
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S283 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) IIb C - A ‘distal-shifting’ strategy may be considered in any TAAA to reduce left lung manipulation to a minimum
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S284 care_pathway / workflow_recommendation L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) IIb C - For patients at high risk for SCI undergoing endovascular treatment of type I, II, III or V thoraco-abdominal aortic aneurysms, a staged TEVAR–B/FEVAR approach should be considered
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S285 recommendation / general_recommendation L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) IIa C - In open TAAA repair, proximal clamping before the full establishment of CPB to avoid retrograde embolization of parietal thrombi should be considered
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S286 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) IIa C - Continued In patients undergoing open descending or thoraco-abdominal aorta repair, cryoablation of multiple intercostal spaces (temporary nerve blockage) may be considered for pain control as an adjuvant strategy
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S287 recommendation / general_recommendation L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) IIb C - Left heart bypass or partial femoral– femoral bypass for open thoracoabdominal aorta repair should be considered based on surgical and institutional experience
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S288 recommendation / contextual_statement L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) Selective kidney protection with cold saline or Bretschneider’s solution can be considered [444]
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S289 recommendation / contextual_statement L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) Nowadays, next to the immediate short-term goal of prevention of aneurysm rupture, the long-term outcomes of each treatment strategy should be carefully taken into consideration
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S290 recommendation / contextual_statement L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) A patient’s and a surgeon’s decision to proceed with elective treatment should be preceded by careful preoperative assessment of concomitant disease and general health status (e.g
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S291 recommendation / contextual_statement L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) A midline transperitoneal or retroperitoneal surgical AAA access route should be based on the surgeon’s experience, preference and patient-specific aspects
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S292 recommendation / contextual_statement L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) angulation, tortuosity) is required for optimal sealing and fixation
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S293 recommendation / contextual_statement L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) In such cases, patients and surgeons should be aware of the associated increased complication and overall mortality rates [666–668]
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S294 recommendation / contextual_statement L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) Coil embolization of the aneurysm sac or the inferior mesenteric artery may be beneficial to prevent type II endoleak [671, 672]
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S295 recommendation / general_recommendation L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) I C - In patients with complicated PAU, dissection intramural haematoma, or pseudoaneurysm with suitable anatomy of the abdominal aorta, endovascular repair should be considered as the first option
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S296 care_pathway / workflow_recommendation L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) IIa C - In patients with intact AAA with suitable anatomy for either open or EVAR, a shared decision-making process for each approach, including life expectancy, is recommended
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S297 recommendation / general_recommendation L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) I C - In patients with long life expectancy, open AAA repair should be considered the preferred treatment modality
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S298 recommendation / general_recommendation L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) IIa B [651–654] In patients with suitable anatomy and reasonable life expectancy, endovascular AAA repair should be considered the preferred treatment modality
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S299 recommendation / general_recommendation L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) IIa B [651–654] In patients with ruptured AAA undergoing endovascular treatment, a bifurcated device should be considered as preferable over an aorta-uniiliac device if anatomically suitable
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S300 recommendation / contextual_statement L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) In these patients, cross-clamping is required above 1 or 2 of the renal arteries with or without selective renal perfusion
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S301 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) In all patients with AAA, a proximal anastomosis should be performed as close as possible to the lowest renal artery
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S302 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) Next to the administration of adequate intravenous antibiotic regimens to treat the aortic and aetiologic systemic infections, surgical intervention should be performed irrespective of aneurysm size [690, 691]
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S303 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) The sacrifice of 1 IIA may be considered if the revascularization is technically challenging (e.g
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S304 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) / European Journal of Cardio-Thoracic Surgery Recommendation : First branch vessels Recommendations Classa Levelb Refc In patients with involvement of the iliac axis in the disease process, the preservation of blood flow to at least 1 hypogastric artery is recommended
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S305 recommendation / general_recommendation L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) I B [695] In patients with isolated iliac artery aneurysms, elective treatment should be considered at a diameter threshold of a minimum of 35 mm
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S306 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) IIa B [696, 697] In patients with isolated iliac artery aneurysms, endovascular aneurysm repair may be considered
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S307 recommendation / contextual_statement L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) In the case of the embolization and coverage of 1 IIA, the contralateral vessel should be preserved
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S308 recommendation / contextual_statement L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) Proximal coiling of the IIA trunk should be preferred rather than selective embolization of the branches
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S309 recommendation / contextual_statement L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) Type I endoleaks have a high risk of rupture and should be treated at the time of diagnosis [718]
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S310 recommendation / contextual_statement L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) Type IIIa endoleaks affecting aortoiliac endografts have a sizeable complication rate and should therefore be repaired promptly
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S311 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) The Recommendation : Therapeutic options: endoleaks Recommendations Classa Levelb Refc Type I and type III endoleaks are regarded as treatment failures, and reintervention is recommended
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S312 recommendation / general_recommendation L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) I C - In the presence of a type II endoleak and aneurysm growth >_10 mm, treatment should be considered in both thoracic and abdominal pathologies
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S313 recommendation / general_recommendation L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) IIa C - In the presence of a type V endoleak (endotension without depictable endoleak on standard follow-up imaging), treatment should be considered in both thoracic and abdominal pathologies
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S314 recommendation / contextual_statement L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) Consequently, retrograde perfusion of the false lumen from the aortic branches or from the entry tears downstream from the distal endograft should not be referred to as endoleaks [128]
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S315 recommendation / contextual_statement L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) Furthermore, the reperfusion of the false lumen through the membrane rupture at the distal endograft end should be described as the dSINE and not as an endoleak [128]
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S316 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) IIa C - Continued In patients with a prosthetic vascular graft, with positive blood cultures without a clear source, multimodality imaging (CTA, 18F-FDG PET/CT) to evaluate potential graft infection is recommended
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S317 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) I B [729–731] In patients with vascular graft infection who are fit for surgery, open surgical repair is recommended
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S318 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) I C - In patients with vascular graft infection and new-onset pseudoaneurysm or ongoing bleeding, emergency operative management is recommended
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S319 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) I C - In patients with vascular graft infection and active bleeding into the airway or gastrointestinal tract, endovascular treatment may be considered as a bridging strategy
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S320 recommendation / general_recommendation L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) IIb C - In patients with vascular graft infection who are haemodynamically stable without vascular discontinuity, scheduled open surgery should be considered
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S321 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) IIa C - Surgical management Surgical management of vascular graft infection, including excision of prosthetic material, local debridement and preferably orthotopic vascular reconstruction, is recommended
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S322 recommendation / general_recommendation L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) The timing of surgery for vascular graft infections in In patients with suspected vascular graft infection undergoing open surgical repair, multiple samples for obtaining conventional culture, 16S RNA genomic analysis and pathological examination are recommended
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S323 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) I C - For surgical vascular graft infection management, liberal use of adjunct therapies such as delayed cavity closure, use of coverage flaps and continuous irrigation may be considered based on individual characteristics and local expertise
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S324 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) IIb C - Postsurgical management In patients who were operated on for vascular graft infection, a postoperative antimicrobial regimen for at least 6 weeks is recommended, if the tissue samples from retrieved specimens are culture positive
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S325 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) I C - In patients with intraoperative purulence, MRSA, or infection caused by a multidrug-resistant microorganism or Candida spp, a 6-month regimen of antimicrobial therapy is recommended
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S326 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) In selected patients, life-long antibiotic therapy is recommended
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S327 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) Coxiella brunetti, Mycobacterium spp, Mycoplasma spp, Bartonella spp) extending the postoperative antimicrobial course beyond 6 weeks may be considered
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S328 recommendation / general_recommendation L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) IIb C - 18F-PET/CT should be considered to decide on the duration of prolonged antimicrobial course for vascular graft infection, particularly in patients with a retained vascular graft
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S329 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) IIa C - In patients with vascular graft infection due to a low-virulence causative agent and favourable clinical/imaging features, medical management alone and close follow-up may be considered
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S330 recommendation / contextual_statement L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) Multiple surgical specimens should be retrieved and labelled at the time of surgery for pathological examination and microbiological processing (including genomic sequencing to increase diagnostic yield)
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S331 recommendation / contextual_statement L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) The therapeutic option should be individualized according to the severity and extension of infection and the general condition of the patient
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S332 recommendation / contextual_statement L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) For such fistulas, temporary TEVAR coverage should not be utilized as a sole therapeutic strategy [754]
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S333 recommendation / contextual_statement L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) This as stated, should only be contemplated as a bailout in exceptional situations
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S334 recommendation / contextual_statement L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) In the absence of further evidence surgeons within multidisciplinary teams should apply their best treatment choice, considering their experience, patient’s age, and clinical condition
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S335 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) For large periaortic abscesses or collections, the option of preoperative percutaneous drainage to decrease the bacterial burden may be considered [759]
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S336 recommendation / contextual_statement L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) Discontinuation of antibiotic treatment should be closely monitored
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S337 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) Most commonly, the aberrant subclavian artery passes posterior to the Recommendation : Rare conditions: Kommerell diverticulum Recommendations Classa Levelb Refc In symptomatic patients with a high suspicion (dysphagia) of an aberrant subclavian artery and/or KD, CTA or MRI is recommended
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S338 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) I C - It is recommended to offer multiple measurements of the KD and the adjacent aorta to improve surveillance and facilitate operative decision-making
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S339 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) I C - In symptomatic patients with aberrant subclavian artery and/or KD, treatment is recommended
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S340 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) I C - In patients with aberrant subclavian artery and/or KD, treatment may be considered when the diverticulum orifice is >_30 mm and/or when the combined diameter of the diverticulum and the adjacent aorta (DAW) is >_50 mm and/or when the diameter of the aberrant artery is >_30 mm
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S341 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) IIb C - In patients with a symptomatic aberrant subclavian artery, transsection of the vascular ring and transposition or bypass is recommended to release compression and to re-establish arterial circulation
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S342 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) I C - In young patients without significant comorbidities, open surgical treatment (via a thoracotomy and carotid-to-subclavian artery bypass) of KD is recommended
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S343 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) I C - In patients with an emergency presentation or who might not tolerate open surgery, hybrid (closed-chest) repair of the KD is recommended
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S344 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) I C - In patients with KD and indication for treatment, open surgical resection and endovascular repair with subclavian debranching may be considered depending on the patient’s anatomical characteristics, age and general status
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S345 recommendation / general_recommendation L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) For consistency, multiple measurements are recommended (Fig
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S346 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) In patients with a KD, preventive surgery is recommended when the risk of surgery is lower than the risk of rupture and/or dissection
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S347 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) Open aortic replacement of the aorta may be considered based 63 M
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S348 recommendation / contextual_statement L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) In symptomatic patients (dysphagia, dyspnoea) operative management should guarantee full decompression of the involved surrounding structures
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S349 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) TAR via the FET procedure may be considered in the presence of concomitant aortic arch dilatation [779]
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S350 care_pathway / workflow_recommendation L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) Endovascular approaches may be considered in patients with suitable anatomy, particularly in patients with increased operative risk
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S351 recommendation / contextual_statement L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) In young patients, given the relatively low incidence of comorbidities, open repair through a thoracotomy is preferred due to its durability and efficacy in symptom relief [774, 776]
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S352 recommendation / contextual_statement L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) Patients with CoA should undergo TTE for initial assessment [786, 787] because this modality provides functional and anatomical information regarding site, structure and extent of CoA, LV function and the presence of hypertrophy and concomitant cardiac abnormalities
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S353 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) In the case of suboptimal acoustic windows, cardiac MRI [789, 790] is recommended
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S354 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) In addition, head CTA is recommended for exclusion of intracranial aneurysms, which are often present in patients with CoA and account for 5% of deaths [792]
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S355 recommendation / contextual_statement L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) Magnetic resonance imaging techniques are a useful tool in reducing life-long radiation while providing adequate spatiotemporal resolution and can substitute CTA
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S356 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) Before the introduction of surgical treatment, the mean age of death was 34 years, with a quarter of patients dying Recommendation : Rare conditions: coarctation of the aorta Recommendations Classa Levelb Refc In patients with a diagnosis of CoA, primary or recurrent and with clinical significance, treatment is recommended
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S357 recommendation / general_recommendation L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) I C - Asymptomatic patients with a diagnosis of CoA but in whom there is a systolic gradient across the stenosis at rest (>20 mmHg) and/ or SBP difference above 10 mmHg combined with coarctation-attributed HF and/or proximal hypertension should be considered for open or endovascular repair
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S358 recommendation / general_recommendation L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) IIa C - Regardless of the pressure gradient, patients with documented HTA with >50% aortic luminal narrowing in relation to the aortic diameter at the level of the diaphragm (based on MRI, CT or invasive angiography measurements) should be considered for treatment
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S359 recommendation / general_recommendation L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) IIa C - For adult patients with a diagnosis of CoA, referral to a specialized aortic centre should be considered
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S360 recommendation / general_recommendation L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) IIa C - In patients with a diagnosis of coarctation of the native thoracic aorta as well as after previous repair and suitable anatomy, endovascular repair should be considered as an alternative to open surgical repair
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S361 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) IIa C - In patients requiring coarctation re-repair, extra anatomical bypass from the ascending to the descending aorta may be considered at the time of concomitant cardiac repair
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S362 recommendation / general_recommendation L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) Computed tomography and 18F-FDG PET are useful for the diagnosis of suspected inflammatory disease of the aorta and for the evaluation of therapy during the follow-up period, all this being widely recommended [141]
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S363 recommendation / contextual_statement L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) The indications for peripheral vascular reconstruction in patients with TA must be carefully individualized [851]
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S364 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) Considering the patterns of presentation, imaging follow-up is recommended for all patients with GCA and other inflammatory ADs [866]
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S365 recommendation / contextual_statement L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) however, one should keep in mind that in-hospital mortality is significant [881]
