EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S366 recommendation / contextual_statement L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) The lower rates of aneurysms, especially at the level of the abdominal aorta, should be tempered by the fact that a similar aortic diameter threshold (of 30 mm) in both genders may underestimate the actual prevalence of aneurysms in women
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S367 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) / European Journal of Cardio-Thoracic Surgery Recommendation : Aortic disease in women: pregnancy and post partum Recommendations Classa Levelb Refc Delivery in pregnant patients with aortopathy In pregnant patients with a history of aortic dissection, C-section is recommended
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S368 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) I C - In pregnant patients with an aortic diameter of <40 mm, vaginal delivery is recommended
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S369 recommendation / general_recommendation L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) I C - C-section should be considered in pregnant patients with a diameter >_45 mm of the aortic root or ascending aorta
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S370 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) IIa C - Surgery before pregnancy in women with aortic disease Aortic repair before pregnancy is recommended in patients with Marfan’s syndrome and aortic root diameter >_45 mm
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S371 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) I C - Continued With an aortic root diameter of 40–45 mm, aortic repair before pregnancy may be considered in the presence of risk factors for aortic dissection (family history or rapid growth >3 mm/year)
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S372 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) IIb C - Pregnancy in patients with aortopathy: dissection and surgery in pregnancy Urgent aortic repair is recommended with foetal monitoring in patients with acute type A aortic dissection during the first 2 trimesters
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S373 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) I C - In patients with acute type A aortic dissection during the third trimester, urgent caesarean delivery immediately followed by aortic surgery is recommended
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S374 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) I C - OMT is recommended in patients with acute type B aortic dissection during pregnancy unless complications develop
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S375 recommendation / contextual_statement L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) This difference has opened the debate on whether a similar diameter threshold should be used to decide intervention in women and in men [894]
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S376 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) It is recommended that women with a risk for aortopathy having a planned pregnancy undergo imaging including echocardiography, MRI or CT, to elucidate the entire vascular condition
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S377 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) Surveillance imaging throughout pregnancy and several weeks post partum using ultrasound is recommended to monitor for congenital changes in aortic size
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S378 recommendation / general_recommendation L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) In women with one of the previously mentioned hereditary conditions, or a known aortopathy, pre-pregnancy genetic counselling, baseline aortic imaging and understanding of the potential risk of dissection with the aim of shared decision-making with the patient are recommended
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S379 recommendation / contextual_statement L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) In the grey zone between 40 mm and 45 mm of root dimension, shared decision-making with a patient is required [907, 908], because recommendations are not clear: The World Health Organization classification for cardiovascular risk for women with Marfan syndrome and moderate aortic dilation is risk class III, whereas those with an aortic root diameter of >45 mm are considering to be in risk class IV [909]
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S380 recommendation / general_recommendation L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) Because of the increased risk of aortic dissection, pregnancy should be avoided when the aortic root is >45 mm and pre-emptive repair should be considered
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S381 recommendation / contextual_statement L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) Patients at risk in their families should realize a slightly enhanced risk of contracting aortic dissection, even in the post-partum period
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S382 recommendation / contextual_statement L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) In the planning phase of any pre-emptive aortic surgery, the risk and the potential benefit should be discussed in detail, with operations ranging from a valve-sparing root replacement operation such as a David 5 and the Yacoub procedure, to a rapid procedure involving the aorta according to the Paediatric Emergency Assessment, Recognition, and Stabilization concept
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S383 recommendation / contextual_statement L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) Aortic dissection and aortic surgery in pregnancy Usually during pregnancy, if marked aortic dilation is present or rapid aortic diameter progression >3 mm is noted, a certain risk of maternal aortic dissection should be discussed
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S384 recommendation / contextual_statement L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) decision-making process and should be individualized based on maternal and foetal risk assessment
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S385 recommendation / contextual_statement L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) A dedicated care team usually located at an AC should be involved
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S386 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) If type A aortic dissection occurs in the first 26 weeks of pregnancy, emergency cardiac surgery should be performed, recognizing the risk of foetal loss [388, 903]
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S387 recommendation / contextual_statement L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) In the aggregate, prophylactic aortic surgery during pregnancy requires a complex decision-making process, and management should be individualized based on maternal and foetal risk and benefits
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S388 recommendation / contextual_statement L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) ‘As low as reasonably achievable’ principles to protect patient and team members should be applied by physicians performing aortic interventions [916]
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S389 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) I C - Normothermia is recommended in the early postoperative phase
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S390 recommendation / general_recommendation L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) I C - The femoral artery should be considered as preferred for arterial pressure monitoring postoperatively
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S391 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) IIa C - Close monitoring of lactate levels as a surrogate for postoperative malperfusion is recommended
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S392 recommendation / general_recommendation L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) I C - Point-of-care monitoring, in addition to regular laboratory coagulation parameters, should be considered in the early postoperative phase after open aortic repair
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S393 recommendation / contextual_statement L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) Therefore, femoral arterial pressure monitoring should be preferred in the early postoperative phase
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S394 recommendation / general_recommendation L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) Pulmonary artery catheter or continuous cardiac output monitoring using arterial waveform analysis should be considered as first-line monitoring measures
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S395 recommendation / contextual_statement L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) Due to the interaction of temperature and the coagulation system, respectively, and the higher rate of blood loss, postoperative inadvertent hypothermia should be avoided, and patients should be kept normothermic in the postoperative phase [925]
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S396 recommendation / contextual_statement L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) In general, clinical assessment should address respiratory, cardiovascular, hepatic, coagulation, renal and neurologic systems
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S397 recommendation / contextual_statement L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) Neurologic evaluation should be facilitated as soon as possible after aortic repair by weaning the patient from sedatives and enabling spontaneous ventilation
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S398 recommendation / contextual_statement L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) Adherence to an enhanced recovery protocol after surgery should be pursued, including early weaning from sedatives and transferring the patient to an intermittent mandatory ventilation mode
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S399 recommendation / contextual_statement L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) Once the patient is admitted to the ICU, laboratory as well as point-of-care (POC) coagulation monitoring (thromboelastography) is required in order to maintain haemostasis and detect haemostatic abnormalities early
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S400 recommendation / contextual_statement L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) In accordance with the 2017 EACTS/EACTA Guidelines for Patient Blood Management, transfusion strategies should be implemented [922, 931]
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S401 recommendation / general_recommendation L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) Liberal substitution of fibrinogen should be recommended in patients with low fibrinogen levels and evidence of postoperative microvascular bleeding
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S402 recommendation / general_recommendation L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) In terms of transfusion triggers for platelet concentrates in bleeding patients, a cut-off Rescue protocols for postoperative SCI after aortic arch surgery procedures that include CSF drainage and blood pressure elevation are recommended
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S403 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) I C - Immediate CSF drainage in patients with new onset of paraplegia after TEVAR or open TAAA replacement is recommended
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S404 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) I C - For patients with ILT after FET procedures, therapeutic anticoagulation is recommended
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S405 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) I C - Early TEVAR extension may be considered in patients with ILT after a FET implant
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S406 recommendation / general_recommendation L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) Regular clinical assessments are recommended [933]
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S407 recommendation / contextual_statement L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) In cases of SCI, patients should at least be treated with CSF drainage for 5 days to allow for reorganization of the arterial collateral spinal network [949]
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S408 recommendation / contextual_statement L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) Patients treated with CSF drainage should be kept in a lying position to avoid intracranial pressure drop, possibly resulting in a subdural haematoma
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S409 recommendation / contextual_statement L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) ILT was a predictor of early mortality in 1 study and should be treated with therapeutic anticoagulation
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S410 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) Optimal medical treatment of blood pressure is recommended in all types of AD and should always be part of the postoperative management
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S411 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) The use of ß-blockers should be considered as a first-line treatment along with the implementation of calcium channel antagonists and/or renin-angiotensin inhibitors, if required [324]
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S412 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) In both patient cohorts, slightly higher systolic blood pressure (mean arterial pressure 80–90 mmHg) and haemoglobin levels of 10 mg/dl may be considered up to the exclusion of a neurologic deficit and according to the individual patient’s risk for paraplegia [958]
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S413 recommendation / contextual_statement L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) In case of paraplegia, higher blood pressure levels should be continued, and placement of a CSF drain should follow immediately after correction of the coagulopathy
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S414 recommendation / contextual_statement L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) Especially in acute complicated TBAD, the ICU team should be aware of the characteristics of the true lumen and perfusion of the peripheral arteries before and after the intervention in order to estimate the individual patient’s risk for organ ischaemia and to initiate specific examinations and treatment
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S415 recommendation / contextual_statement L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) Pulse deficit and peripheral perfusion should be evaluated in both legs to estimate the level of true lumen collapse or thrombosis at the iliac artery axis and more distally
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S416 recommendation / general_recommendation L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) Computed contrast enhanced tomography or invasive angiography in hybrid operating room settings should be considered in order to verify the diagnosis and to initiate immediate treatment
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S417 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) In the ICU setting, echocardiography is recommended to exclude or detect the presence of a dissection flap in the proximal aorta and pericardial effusion in case of chest pain, haemodynamic depression and stroke followed by CT angiography
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S418 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) LIVING WITH AORTIC DISEASE Recommendation : Living with aortic disease Recommendations Classa Levelb Refc Exercise and sports Thorough education regarding the individual risks and benefits of exercise is recommended
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S419 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) I C - For patients with adequately controlled BP, 30–60 min of mild-to-moderate dynamic exercise at least 3–4 days per week is recommended
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S420 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) III C - Cardiac rehabilitation In patients after invasive treatment of aortic pathologies, an individual cardiac rehabilitation programme under medical supervision is recommended
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S421 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) I C - Surveillance In any patients receiving treatment of aortic pathologies, a diseaseand treatment-based individual surveillance programme in a specialized aortic centre with a dedicated outpatient clinic is recommended
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S422 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) I C - Imaging-based quality control after every open or endovascular aortic procedure is recommended, irrespective of the treated segment before discharge
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S423 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) I C - In patients after treatment for acute aortic dissection or IMH irrespective of treatment modality, CTA surveillance is recommended after 6months and 12months and, in case of stable conditions, annually thereafter for 5years
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S424 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) I C - In patients after endovascular treatment irrespective of the underlying aortic disease, CTA surveillance is recommended after 6months and 12months and, in case of stable conditions, annually thereafter for 5years
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S425 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) I C - In patients after open surgical treatment for non-aortic dissection or IMH pathologies, imaging surveillance is recommended after 12 months and 24 months and, in case of stable conditions, should be extended thereafter
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S426 recommendation / general_recommendation L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) I C - In patients with stable aortic conditions, extended surveillance intervals should be considered after 5 years based on an individual protocol
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S427 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) IIa C - In case of non-operability, no option or informed consent of refraining from treatment, stopping surveillance is recommended
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S428 recommendation / contextual_statement L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) A patient with connective tissue disease involving the aorta or one already diagnosed with an aortic pathology should be individually advised by clinicians by expounding upon the positive and negative effects of exercise on the given AD
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S429 recommendation / contextual_statement L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) A major effect of sports is obviously the positive effect on quality of life—a factor of utmost importance, especially in patients diagnosed with inherent AD who are still burdened by stigmatization and social difficulties that should not be exacerbated by the unnecessary prohibition of physical activity
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S430 recommendation / contextual_statement L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) This issue must be discussed openly to enable shared decision-making with the patient, parents and coach(es)
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S431 recommendation / contextual_statement L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) Nevertheless, a patient with an aortic aneurysm or who has had an aortic dissection should be encouraged to engage in sports
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S432 recommendation / contextual_statement L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) Because the maximum increase in blood pressure occurs at the level when an additional repetition cannot be carried out, irrespective of the amount of weight, patients with AD should only use low weights and stop exercise well before muscle fatigue occurs [968]
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S433 recommendation / therapeutic_action L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) Nevertheless, close surveillance via a low-static and low-dynamic individual programme overseen by an experienced team is recommended
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S434 recommendation / contextual_statement L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) Patients should therefore discuss returning to work with their physician or surgeon regarding time points and the need for additional surveillance
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S435 recommendation / contextual_statement L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) The CR programme should be started as early as possible and carried out under professional medical supervision in close cooperation with the primary treating AC [974]
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S436 recommendation / contextual_statement L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) Nevertheless, as a general rule, the more untreated segments there are remaining, the more frequently the patient should be seen, whereas there is a gradient from high-risk patients (dissection patients with endovascular or medical treatment) to low-risk patients (aneurysm patients after open repair) for subsequent aortic events that should be taken into account for planning an individual surveillance programme in each patient
EACTS-STS-AORTIC-SYNDROMES-2024:S437 recommendation / contextual_statement L1_structured / pending pending / pending candidate / high critical (1.00) Several areas have been identified where more research is needed, gaps that the global aortic community should strive to address: • Identifying diagnostic biomarkers for confirming aortic dissection
